races.race_name: Welcome To The Perth Festival Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Newbrook Diamond
runners.spotlight: Point winner who made it 0-6 over hurdles when 26l seventh of 25 at Fairyhouse recently in first-time blinkers (retained); however, he's been placed four times in this sphere and will be a major player for his top Irish trainer if back to his best this afternoon.
runners.comment: 0-6 over hurdles and below par latest, but placed 4 times and major player if back to best

runners.name: Gee Force Flyer
runners.spotlight: Point winner who has finished in the first three on all four hurdle starts (soft/good to soft), most recently third on handicap debut in the competitive EBF Final at Sandown (2m4f); strong claims on the back of that.
runners.comment: Third of 17 in the competitive EBF Final at Sandown and strong claims on the back of that

runners.name: Off The Jury
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in Haydock handicap in February to make it 0-12 under rules; had wind operations before and after that latest run; arrives with something to prove but he has lots of good placed form and is a leading contender if back on song; the tongue-tie adopted last time is left off.
runners.comment: Pulled up last time to make it 0-12 under rules but leading claims judged on peak efforts

runners.name: Holy Joe
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; half-brother to winners Miss Aloud (2m4f hurdle for these connections; RPR 118) and Oak Bluffs (7f-1m4f Flat); dam unraced; no surprise to see a big run on debut; top Irish trainer also runs the experienced Newbrook Diamond and the betting could be informative.
runners.comment: Top Irish trainer also runs the experienced Newbrook Diamond; check the betting on debut

runners.name: Expensive Emotion
runners.spotlight: Won Irish mares' maiden point (3m, yielding to soft) last November on her second start and sold for £40,000 in December; new trainer is having an excellent season and she could have a part to play on rules debut.
runners.comment: Bought for £40,000 after point win; new trainer is having an excellent season

runners.name: Prince Phil
runners.spotlight: Fairly encouraging fifth at Kelso in February in his sole bumper but pulled up/tailed off at triple-figure odds on both hurdle starts.
runners.comment: Fairly encouraging fifth in sole bumper but pulled up/tailed off on both hurdle starts

runners.name: Eagle's Cairn
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability in a bumper and on hurdle debut but pulled up over hurdles on his last three starts and he would be a surprise winner.
runners.comment: Pulled up on his last three starts and he would be a surprise winner

runners.name: Heffalump
runners.spotlight: From in-form yard and he's a half-brother to the useful Kiltealy Briggs; he's had wind surgery since his debut in a Carlisle bumper (2m1f, soft) last November but was tailed off at 16-1 and a transformation is needed.
runners.comment: Half-brother to useful Kiltealy Briggs but tailed off on sole bumper start; wind op since

runners.name: Run Baby Run
runners.spotlight: 200-1 or bigger for all five runs; pulled up/tailed off each time.
runners.comment: 200-1 or bigger for all five runs; pulled up/tailed off each time

races.race_name: Balnakeilly Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Walking On A Dream
runners.spotlight: Clear second to highly progressive winner at Hexham (2m4f, heavy) in December and returned to form for 9l win in three-runner race at Ayr (2m4f, soft) last month; has also shown promise on good to soft ground; up in grade today but he's in good hands to continue to progress; respected.
runners.comment: Won three-runner race at Ayr; up in grade but he's in good hands to continue to progress

runners.name: Barlovento
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten on last two starts but those were Class 2 contests at Cheltenham and Kempton and this is a drop back in grade; this lightly raced 7yo had previously made a promising start to his chasing career, including a 2m4f win at Kempton, and he remains one to be interested in.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten on last two starts but in tougher races; he remains one to be interested in

runners.name: Find A Fortune
runners.spotlight: 9yo who is lightly raced for his age; he needs to produce more than he managed at Leopardstown and Downpatrick last month on his first two chase starts but he wears first-time headgear today and Gordon Elliott's horses are always well worth a second look at Perth; could have a big say.
runners.comment: Improvement needed on third chase start but yard's horses always worth a second look here

runners.name: It's Hard To Know
runners.spotlight: Point (3m, soft to heavy) and two-time novice hurdle (2m3f/2m4f, good/good to soft) winner; he's run just once this season, when 14l fourth of six on chase debut at Aintree (2m4f, soft) in December; however, he remains very lightly raced and is certainly not written off today.
runners.comment: Just one run this season, when soundly beaten on chase debut, but he's not written off

runners.name: King Roly
runners.spotlight: Made a good start to his chasing career last year, winning three times between May and September (2m3f/2m4f, good), with two of them over C&D; also effective on good to soft, unproven on softer; he has a very solid record here and could play a leading role if fully tuned up after a break.
runners.comment: Has a very solid record here and could play a leading role if fully tuned up after a break

runners.name: Karafon
runners.spotlight: Runner-up when back over fences at Ayr (2m4f, soft) in January and this 6yo is of interest on that form; however, three lesser performances have followed and he needs to raise his game if he's to win this.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Ayr in January but three lesser efforts have followed; has to raise his game

runners.name: Great Pepper
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 8yo who was runner-up at Ayr (2m, soft) in January when dropped back in trip on his second chase start; however, pulled up at Carlisle (2m, good to soft) since, which severely dents confidence in his claims today, and the step up in distance may not be ideal.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Ayr in January but pulled up at Carlisle since and needs to bounce back

races.race_name: British EBF Gold Castle 'National Hunt' Novices' Hurdle (Listed Race) (GBB Race)

runners.name: No Drama This End
runners.spotlight: Made it 3-3 over hurdles (2m5f/2m4f, soft/good) in ready fashion in the Grade 1 Challow at Newbury in December; very disappointing favourite in the Turners at the Cheltenham Festival, even allowing for what went wrong at the start, but he's the one to beat today if back to his best; promises to be suited by the step up in trip (dam won 3m French Grade 1).
runners.comment: Grade 1 winner; disappointing favourite at Cheltenham but the one to beat if back to best
runners.owner: McNeill Family And Chris & Giles Barber

runners.name: Tormund Giantsbane
runners.spotlight: No match for the winner No Drama This End when 12l fourth of five in Grade 2 at Sandown (2m4f, soft) in December; however, he showed improved form when easily making all in Doncaster novice (3m, good to soft) in February and can give a good account in his rematch; has also won on soft.
runners.comment: No match for No Drama This End at Sandown but he's improved since; can go well
runners.owner: Cherry Knoll Farm, M Ward & D Skelton

runners.name: Kazansky
runners.spotlight: Won Grade 2 novice hurdle at Limerick (2m7f, heavy) in December and 8l second in Grade 1 at Leopardstown (2m6f, heavy) in February; pulled up at 25-1 in the Albert Bartlett at Cheltenham (3m, soft) last month but back in calmer waters today and he's one to be interested in given his previous promise.
runners.comment: Grade 2 winner; pulled up at Cheltenham but one to be interested in back in calmer waters

runners.name: Sept Etoiles
runners.spotlight: 5yo who has raced four times, all over hurdles, and he was winning for a second time when powering clear in a big-field handicap at Fairyhouse (3m, yielding to soft) this month; today is a tougher test of his credentials but he was impressive last time and could be up to the task.
runners.comment: Impressive in big-field handicap at Fairyhouse this month; respected despite tougher task

runners.name: Sogna In Grande
runners.spotlight: Point winner who is 2-2 over hurdles following novice wins at Warwick (2m5f, soft) and Wincanton (2m5f, good); others have more substance to their form but he did it easily last time and it remains to be seen where his limitations lie.
runners.comment: 2-2 over hurdles; others have stronger form but remains to be seen where limitations lie

races.race_name: Fair Maid Of Perth Mares' Chase (Listed Race) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Panic Attack
runners.spotlight: Ran in the Grand National 11 days ago, badly hampered at the second fence and falling at the third; won two high-profile handicaps last November before Listed win at Newbury and the drop back to 2m4f didn't appear ideal at the Cheltenham Festival; leading claims if none the worse for last time.
runners.comment: Early exit in Grand National 11 days ago; leading claims on the rest of her form this term

runners.name: Shecouldbeanything
runners.spotlight: She'll find it hard to beat a peak-form Panic Attack and she was pulled up in the Irish National 16 days ago; however, she was running well in January, in a Grade 2 mares' chase at Fairyhouse (2m6f, yielding) and the Thyestes Handicap at Gowran Park (3m1f, heavy; albeit no match for the first two); no surprise if she bounces back with a good performance today.
runners.comment: Pulled up in recent Irish National but running well in January and could bounce back today

runners.name: Apple Away
runners.spotlight: Consistent sort who won this two years ago (soft); Panic Attack has better form and she's run just once this season, but that was a solid effort when third of five at Carlisle in November over an inadequate 2m4f (good to soft), and she can give a good account.
runners.comment: Won this two years ago; just one run this season (November) but can give a good account

runners.name: Ruby Island
runners.spotlight: 9yo who has been progressive during this first chasing campaign, winning twice (2m/2m4f, soft/good to soft); she's open to further improvement but it's necessary now back up in grade, and she needs to prove her stamina; soundly beaten over hurdles on sole previous run in these cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Progressive during this first chasing campaign but further improvement is needed

runners.name: Coup De Coeur
runners.spotlight: Can be hit and miss in the jumping department but his profile over fences is generally progressive and he backed up February's Sedgefield romp with good second in fairly valuable series final at Carlisle (also 2m, good to soft) one month ago; respected.
runners.comment: Good second in fairly valuable series final (2m, good to soft) one month ago; progressive

runners.name: Fringill Dike
runners.spotlight: Won four of his first five completed starts over fences (2023/24) but just one success since, a Newton Abbot handicap, and he wasn't finishing his races off last year; 185-day break may have helped but this forecast soft ground raises further doubts.
runners.comment: Rather lost his way; 185-day break may help but forecast soft ground also raises doubts

runners.name: Aeros Luck
runners.spotlight: Another ex-Gavin Cromwell inmate who secured narrow win over fences at Punchestown (2m, good to yielding) in June; 0-5 for this yard but February's close second of five at Musselburgh (2m, good to soft) on penultimate start was a creditable effort; stable in fine form.
runners.comment: February's close second on penultimate start was a creditable effort; stable in fine form

runners.name: Jigoro
runners.spotlight: Blinkered first time when third of 14 on his handicap debut in this sphere at Thurles (2m5f, yielding) in January and off the mark over fences when landing fairly valuable event at Gowran (2m, heavy) last month; 22-1 and pulled up in Listed novice handicap last time but that had the makings of a warm event and he needs respecting.
runners.comment: 31P in handicap chases, the pulled up probably best ignored; unexposed and needs respect

runners.name: Heltenham
runners.spotlight: All chase wins around 2m4f, including win at this meeting last year; Newbury specialist who landed fourth win at that track when bagging valuable handicap for second time on penultimate start; never won off a mark this high and drop back down to 2m also asks a question but he's impossible to rule out.
runners.comment: Mark and drop in trip ask a question but he's impossible to rule out
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late N W Lake

runners.name: Sunnyvilla
runners.spotlight: Veteran of the field but he won over 2m with plenty of give underfoot on first and fifth races for this yard, latterly at Uttoxeter in January on penultimate start; 2m4f a plausible excuse last time and he needs consideration.
runners.comment: Veteran but 2-6 for this yard and 2m4f a plausible excuse last time; needs consideration

runners.name: The Other Mozzie
runners.spotlight: Three chase wins for Gavin Cromwell included comfortable win in good event at Ayr (2m, good to soft) one year ago; fluffed the start on stable debut in Cheltenham's Grand Annual but had yet to be asked for his effort when falling three out last month; now back down to that Ayr mark, while the first-time visor and booking of Harry Cobden are other points of interest.
runners.comment: Yard debut ignored; on last winning mark with first-time visor & Harry Cobden taking over

runners.name: Flying Fortune
runners.spotlight: Won four on the trot, all at around 2m4f, in 2024 (good/soft); was then absent for 484 days; needs to bounce back having suffered two heavy defeats this season.
runners.comment: Won four on trot in 2024; was then absent for 484 days and two heavy defeats since

runners.name: Fresh As A Daisy
runners.spotlight: Uttoxeter mares' novice winner last May (2m4f, good); placed in two similar races late last year but pulled up on handicap debut in a hot race at Newbury last month; this easier; worth considering.
runners.comment: Mares' novice winner last May; pulled up latest, but down in grade and one to consider

runners.name: Atlantic City
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; creditable second in a maiden hurdle at Haydock (1m7f, good) on stable debut earlier this month; should build on that; longer trip and softer ground no problem; interesting candidate.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish; 2nd in maiden hurdle on stable debut; 2m4f on soft no problem; interesting one

runners.name: Spit Spot
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 3m here last autumn (good/soft); hasn't been in such good form since but may fare better back here with cheekpieces now tried.
runners.comment: Dual winner over 3m here last autumn; not been in such good form since; cheekpieces tried

runners.name: Tread Softly Now
runners.spotlight: Ayr winner in November (2m3f, soft); some fair form in lower grade events than this since; visor replaces normal cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Ayr winner in November; some fair form since; stiffer task now; visor replaces cheekpieces

runners.name: Just An Artist
runners.spotlight: Won a 2m maiden hurdle here on soft last October; mainly creditable efforts since, including when third in mares' handicap at Musselburgh last time (2m4f, good to soft; fourth has won since); chance.
runners.comment: Maiden hurdle winner here on soft; good run in mares' handicap over 2m4f last time; chance

runners.name: Pitwood Road
runners.spotlight: Two good runs on first two starts for Nicky Richards, including Carlisle win off 9lb lower in December (2m3f, soft); had stiff task last time; back down in grade; chance.
runners.comment: Two good runs for current yard last year; stiff task latest; chance back down in grade

races.race_name: Equine Products Best Turned Out Amateur Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle (Div I)

runners.name: Jeteye
runners.spotlight: Only win was over 2m7f at Hexham in June 2024 (good); fair third at Wetherby last time (3m, good); chance on his best form but soft ground not ideal.
runners.comment: Hexham winner in 2024; fair third at Wetherby last time but soft ground not ideal

runners.name: Rioja Alta
runners.spotlight: Winner off 3lb lower at Cartmel last summer (2m6f, soft); two good runs this year, latest when third in a stronger race at Wetherby (3m, good to soft); chance down in grade.
runners.comment: Cartmel winner on soft last year; two good runs over 3m in 2026; down in grade; chance

runners.name: Follow Charlie
runners.spotlight: Has won four times over hurdles, including over 2m4f here last May (good); softer ground no problem but stamina to prove.
runners.comment: Four-time hurdle winner, including over 2m4f here; going suits, but stamina to prove

runners.name: Bollin Matilda
runners.spotlight: Consistent mare who got off the mark for the season in a valuable race at Musselburgh last time (3m, good to soft); chance off this 4lb higher mark.
runners.comment: Off the mark for the season at Musselburgh last time; chance off this 4lb higher mark

runners.name: Coylton King
runners.spotlight: Two best runs have been over 3m at Ayr on last two starts, latest when second of six on soft ground; each-way chance.
runners.comment: Best form on last two starts, both over 3m at Ayr (second of six latest); each-way chance

runners.name: Lennie Godber
runners.spotlight: Relatively unexposed, but mainly modest efforts, including on stable debut at Ayr last time (2m7f, good to soft); may do better.
runners.comment: Unexposed sort who hasn't shown a great deal, including on stable debut last time

runners.name: No Rematch
runners.spotlight: Second of four on stable debut here last May (3m2f, good); hasn't run quite as well since but is now on a career-low mark; blinkers replace cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Second of four over 3m2f here last May but hasn't run quite as well since; new headgear.

runners.name: Suspected
runners.spotlight: Won a bumper for Tom Symonds at Uttoxeter last May (2m, good); fair form over hurdles for this yard, including in two handicaps; needs to improve for step up to 3m.
runners.comment: Won bumper for Tom Symonds last year; fair form over hurdles; needs to improve for 3m

runners.name: Millford Hill
runners.spotlight: Won Irish point in October 2023 (3m, soft/heavy; runner-up has won since); hasn't shown much in maiden/novice hurdles over 2m/2m4f but marked improvement expected now up in trip for handicap debut.
runners.comment: Winning Irish point; ordinary hurdles form; improvement expected up in trip on h'cap debut

runners.name: Pure Sirloin
runners.spotlight: Has a strike-rate of 1-19, but was a solid third at Hexham last time (2m4f, good); just as effective over 3m and acts on soft ground.
runners.comment: 1-19; good third over 2m4f at Hexham latest; just as effective at 3m and soft ground suits

runners.name: Don Brocco
runners.spotlight: Both hurdle wins came over 3m/3m1f on soft back in 2022; fair second in a 4m h'cap chase at Hexham last month (soft), but not so good last time.
runners.comment: Both hurdles wins were in 2022 (3m/3m1f); 2nd in h'cap chase in March; not so good latest

races.race_name: Equine Products Best Turned Out Amateur Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle (Div II)

runners.name: Maillot Blanc
runners.spotlight: C&D winner off 3lb lower last June (good); clear of the remainder when 3.5l third in a decent race at Kelso last time (2m5f, good; winner went on to win 2m Scottish Champion Hurdle); has won on soft; interesting contender.
runners.comment: C&D winner; good third over 2m5f at Kelso last time (form sound); goes on soft; chance

runners.name: Maura Jeanne
runners.spotlight: Strike-rate of 2-30; has a chance on her second at Carlisle last time (3m1f, good to soft) having also finished runner-up at Ayr in January (3m, heavy); each-way chance.
runners.comment: 2-30; two good runs this year, last time finishing second at Carlisle; each-way chance

runners.name: Blue Indigo
runners.spotlight: Fair third in a Newcastle handicap hurdle in January (2m7f, heavy) but well behind Ck O'Mara at Ayr next time (3m, soft); reverts to hurdles after failing to finish in two runs over fences (running creditably on first occasion).
runners.comment: Chance on his Newcastle third in January but well behind Ck O'Mara next time

runners.name: Monsieur Le Fuzz
runners.spotlight: Won a 3m novice handicap hurdle at Ayr in March 2025 (soft; cheekpieces on for first time); best run since when second at Catterick in February (3m1f, good to soft) but well held in valuable race at Musselburgh last time; each-way chance.
runners.comment: Ayr winner in March 2025 and fair second at Catterick in February; each-way chance

runners.name: Ebselysees
runners.spotlight: Won a Huntingdon mares' novice hurdle for Noel Williams in February 2024 (2m3f, good); well held on stable debut after a three-month break last time but should come on for the run.
runners.comment: Mares' novice winner for Noel Williams; well held on stable debut but should improve

runners.name: Kells Priory
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; bettered his first run for Olly Murphy when 3.5l fourth in a 3m handicap at Wincanton last time (good); has won on soft ground; chance.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish; creditable fourth at Wincanton last time; conditions suit; has a chance

runners.name: Palm Beach
runners.spotlight: Worcester winner for Ben Pauling last July (2m7f, good) but has been pulled up on both starts for Victor Thompson since, the first in a point, the second in a handicap hurdle at Newcastle; bit to prove.
runners.comment: Worcester winner for Ben Pauling in 2025; pulled up both runs for this yard; bit to prove

runners.name: Shighness
runners.spotlight: Hasn't won since taking a handicap chase at Catterick in February 2024 (3m1f, soft); not totally disgraced when third in a 3m3f handicap hurdle at Sedgefield two runs ago (soft) but fell when back over fences last time; bit to prove.
runners.comment: Fair third over hurdles two runs ago, but fell when back chasing last time; bit to prove

runners.name: Ck O'Mara
runners.spotlight: Beat Coylton King (runs in the 5.40) at Ayr (3m, soft) last month, but well held off this 5lb higher mark last time; wouldn't rule out.
runners.comment: Ayr winner last Month (second runs in 5.40); well held last time; wouldn't rule out

runners.name: Roi Du Roume
runners.spotlight: Won an Irish point in November (3m, yielding); creditable fourth in a Hereford bumper (2m, soft) on stable debut in January, but not as good in three runs over hurdles since; interesting up in trip on handicap debut on first run since wind surgery.
runners.comment: Irish point winner; not shown much hurdling, but interesting on h'cap debut after wind op

runners.name: Am I Bovvered
runners.spotlight: Unexposed maiden who needs to improve on her sixth in a mares' handicap at Kelso on her handicap debut last time (2m5f, good); can pull hard so step up to 3m may not be ideal and 2lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Unexposed; fair sixth on h'cap debut last time; can pull hard so 3m not sure to suit

runners.name: Tuscan Point
runners.spotlight: Hit the ground running for Adam Kirby and Taryn Langley, scoring easily last month before running well to finish second off this 10lb higher mark ten days later; below that form when fifth last time (again Southwell, 6f, AW) and now switches to turf (0-14) on a rare foray over 5f; regular eyeshields also swapped for cheekpieces so there's some guesswork involved.
runners.comment: Easy win on yard debut (6f, AW); 0-14 on turf & this just a fourth go at 5f; new headgear

runners.name: Fortunate Star
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who also finished third in this last year; has been in good nick since the visor went on, winning a classified at Southwell and Newcastle (February/March) and placed in three handicaps (also 5f, AW) subsequently; can take a grip so today's likely good pace will suit and he gets on well with Conor Whiteley; player.
runners.comment: C&D winner; third in this last year; arrives in form from AW; player

runners.name: Lucius Aurelius
runners.spotlight: 16-race maiden but he's capable and got no run at Southwell (AW) on penultimate start and was poorly drawn at Pontefract (also 5f, good to soft) a fortnight ago; placed on all three starts here and the breakthrough is far from out of the question.
runners.comment: 0-16 but placed all three starts here; excuses on last two starts; breakthrough possible
runners.owner: John Kelly, John McNeill & Kevin Mowatt

runners.name: Birkenhead
runners.spotlight: The old man of the party but he still likes to get on with it and has a good record here; solid comeback second on AW in January wasn't built on but he's back down to the same mark he won this race from last year; that was a fourth C&D win and he has to enter calculations.
runners.comment: Good track record and won this off the same mark last year; firmly in calculations

runners.name: Nacho Nacho Nacho
runners.spotlight: Off the mark for this yard when making most to land Wolverhampton handicap (7f, AW; first-time visor) at the end of last year; weakened over 6f at that track when last seen; sports a new headgear combination for just his third start on turf and second at 5f.
runners.comment: Sports a new headgear combination for just his third start on turf and second at 5f

runners.name: Let's Go Hugo
runners.spotlight: 1-25 on turf but the win did come in 2022 C&D novice and he was beaten only 2l when fourth in this last year; could only plug on from the rear in both starts this term (5f, AW) but there's nothing wrong with this mark and he might pick up some pieces if there's a pace meltdown.
runners.comment: 1-25 on turf but the win was over C&D; solid fourth in this last year; each-way chance

runners.name: Miss Rainbow
runners.spotlight: Four handicap wins last year included one at 5f and another at this track (7f, good); visor reapplied when a creditable third here (6f, good; led) a fortnight ago and she's another who should give it a good go on or near the lead.
runners.comment: Course winner who's won over 5f; creditable third here (6f) latest; much respected

runners.name: Jamie's Choice
runners.spotlight: Dual classified winner on a sound surface at 5f in 2024; went close in C&D handicap (good) on her comeback last term and not much went right in that same race on her reappearance a fortnight ago; drawn on the wing when down the field in this race last year but not without a squeak this time.
runners.comment: Two 5f classified wins on turf in 2024; also has form over C&D; not beyond the pale

runners.name: Barmyblade
runners.spotlight: Won three times on good ground last spring including over C&D (good; 33-1) on his reappearance; lost his way from June onwards and there were few positives when 14th of 15 on his comeback 12 days ago; has dipped below his last winning mark, though.
runners.comment: C&D winner but he seems to have lost his way; mark fine if he can stage a revival

runners.name: Tommy McJohn
runners.spotlight: Won nursery at Naas (good; first-time blinkers) for Michael O'Callaghan in 2024 and was wearing those aids when third of 14 at Dundalk (also 5f, AW) on final start for Ciaran Murphy; no headgear as he starts off for another new yard (his fifth) after three months away; watch for market confidence.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish 5f winner (blinkers); no headgear for yet another stable debut; watch the market

runners.name: Rotokura Belle
runners.spotlight: 5f AW win as a 2yo; doubled her haul when showing bright speed to overcome stall 8 over C&D two weeks ago, finding plenty for pressure; the runner-up has won since and a 3lb rise may not prove beyond her.
runners.comment: Front-running C&D win two weeks ago, beating a subsequent winner; big player up 3lb

runners.name: Naana's Shadow
runners.spotlight: 5f AW winner for Alice Haynes; ran better than the distance beaten suggests on her stable debut at Southwell two weeks ago, short of room inside the final furlong and eased; that was her first start for 171 days and she could prove a different proposition today.
runners.comment: 5f AW winner for Alice Haynes; encouraging stable debut and should be sharper today

runners.name: Moretons
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden; 50-1 for his two starts for this yard, showing better form when seventh (beaten 2.25l) to Rotokura Belle over C&D two weeks ago; 5lb turnaround in the weights may be insufficient.
runners.comment: 2.25l behind Rotokura Belle over C&D two weeks ago; 5lb turnaround may be insufficient

runners.name: Peggy Boo
runners.spotlight: Just minor promise in three novice/maiden runs last year; well-beaten 28-1 shot on last month's handicap/seasonal return (6f, AW); returning to 5f on turf could help but she does have a point to prove.
runners.comment: Low-key return last month (6f, AW); reverting to turf can help but has a point to prove
runners.owner: S Ullyott, T Coyle & P Robinson

runners.name: Victor Cee
runners.spotlight: 6f maiden win for Karl Burke last summer; began life with his new stable with two solid AW efforts but his form dipped at Wolverhampton in January when last seen; drops in class for his return to action (gelded during his absence); unraced on faster than good and drawn widest.
runners.comment: Drops in class and has been gelded; unraced on faster than good; drawn widest

runners.name: Soca Star
runners.spotlight: Consistent over 5f on turf for the Quinns; not at her best when third of five in Wolverhampton novice (6f) on her stable debut 193 days ago; return to 5f in her favour but cheekpieces remain on the sidelines; others safer.
runners.comment: Return to 5f in her favour but headgear is again left off; others look safer

runners.name: Paroda Diva
runners.spotlight: Trained by Stan Moore as a 2yo; won a 7f fillies' maiden (good) last May and ran well at Kempton on her nursery debut in July (7f); ended her 2yo campaign quietly and it was a similar story on her stable debut two weeks ago (6f); drop to 5f the query this time.
runners.comment: Of interest on her best form but that has come over 7f; trip the query this time

runners.name: Come On Over
runners.spotlight: 0-6 for Richard Fahey and Charlie Clover as a 2yo, recording his peak RPR when second of nine, beaten a neck, in a 7f nursery (good to soft; visored first time) at Haydock last September; sold for 16,000 one month later; down in trip for stable debut and goes without headgear.
runners.comment: Best run for former yard came over 7f & in a visor; likely best watched back from a break

races.race_name: Download The Raceday Ready App Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Cranachan
runners.spotlight: Had been gelded and was up in trip when showing a big chunk of improvement to be beaten a neck at Kempton (1m4f, AW; Mythical Valentine just 1l behind) on his reappearance in March; that was just his second start so reasonable to assume there's better to come; turf debut.
runners.comment: Just 1l ahead of Mythical Valentine latest; likely close between them again; turf debut

runners.name: Mythical Valentine
runners.spotlight: Another to show improvement on his reappearance, finishing just 1l behind Cranachan when third at Kempton (1m4f, AW) in March; had to briefly wait for a run that day and there's unlikely to be much between the pair again; turf debut.
runners.comment: Just 1l behind Cranachan latest; might gain his revenge; turf debut

runners.name: Tide Lines
runners.spotlight: 52,000gns yearling; first foal; dam 1m2f winner (RPR 72), half-sister to useful winners Galapiat (1m4f) and Madame Ambassador (1m2f Listed), out of German 1m2f Group 1 winner; belated debut for this 5yo mare and the market should provide some clues.
runners.comment: 52,000gns yearling; belated debut for this 5yo mare; market should provide some clues

runners.name: King Of Roses
runners.spotlight: 5,000euros yearling; 22-1 and it was an inauspicious debut when beaten 49l in 1m Ayr novice (good to soft) in September; has been gelded and goes up in trip for this comeback, both of which might help, but probably one for further down the line.
runners.comment: Beaten 49l on September's 1m debut; gelded and up in trip but likely best watched

runners.name: Lord
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in conditions race (about 1m3f, good) on final start in Germany but he's better known as a hurdler and gained the latest of four wins in that sphere when taking Listed Aintree event (2m1f, soft) in December; wore cheekpieces when well beaten in Grade 1 this month but could go well switched back to the Flat; yard had a treble at Bangor on Saturday.
runners.comment: Won Listed juvenile hurdle in December; could go well on first British Flat start

races.race_name: EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Stella Lucente
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in two 7f maidens at Lingfield (AW), the latest 26 days ago after a 205-day absence; now tackles a race confined to her own sex for the first time and she looks set to make another bold bid.
runners.comment: Runner-up, against male opposition, in two 7f AW maidens at Lingfield; can do better

runners.name: Enduring Story
runners.spotlight: She has finished a well-beaten last in both AW runs this year (7f); switch to turf not enough to tempt.
runners.comment: Poor form in two AW runs this spring; no appeal switched to turf.

runners.name: Soda
runners.spotlight: 4,000euros yearling; ninth foal; sister to 7f/1m winner Crossford (including 2yo/AW/Hong Kong; RPR 97), half-sister to four winners including So Suave (7f-8.5f including 2yo/AW; 96), Star Story (1m4f AW; 82) and Coloane (1m/8.6f; 69); dam 7f 2yo winner (92); enough paper appeal to warrant a market check in this field.
runners.comment: 4,000euros yearling; ninth foal with five winning siblings; market check advised

runners.name: Le Puy
runners.spotlight: 20-1 for her Ayr debut last September (6f, soft) and dropped away to be beaten 22l; should do better in time but others look stronger in this field.
runners.comment: Well-beaten 7th on Ayr debut (6f, soft) last September; bred to do much better in time

runners.name: Cotai Starlight
runners.spotlight: Similar form in two runs, the latest her stable debut 13 days ago (6f, Tapeta); has 5.5l to make up on Got The Booty on that run so improvement is essential.
runners.comment: 5.5l behind Got The Booty on recent stable debut (6f, AW); improvement essential

runners.name: Borjina
runners.spotlight: Encouraging fourth in a 7f novice at Newcastle last September; now tackles her own sex and she returns from seven months off with her stable in fine form; leading player.
runners.comment: Promising 4th at Newcastle seven months ago; returns for a stable in fine form; unexposed
runners.owner: All About York And Whatton Manor Stud

races.race_name: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap

runners.name: Simply Blue
runners.spotlight: Off the mark for James Owen at the 14th attempt when leading late on in a 6f handicap at Wolverhampton 16 days ago; returning to 7f no problem but a 4lb higher mark in a higher grade does ask more of him.
runners.comment: Led late in a 6f AW handicap 16 days ago; 7f no problem but 4lb higher in a better race

runners.name: Vince Le Prince
runners.spotlight: His C&D record of 112153422 shows his affinity for the track and he made a pleasing return to action when second to the reopposing Evocative Spark here two weeks ago (pair clear); should make his presence felt again off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Good record over C&D; sold reappearance behind Evocative Spark two weeks ago; contender

runners.name: Asian Journey
runners.spotlight: Still seeking a first win after 14 attempts but he has been threatening to put that right on AW this year and he ran a solid race back on turf at Doncaster 24 days ago; a strongly run 7f should suit and he has a small race in him off this mark.
runners.comment: Exposed maiden but he's more than capable off this mark if the cards fall right for him

runners.name: Tattie Bogle
runners.spotlight: Still seeking a first handicap success but he went desperately close at Kempton last month (7f); not quite so good back at Kempton two weeks ago; well drawn for a prominent racer; each-way shout.
runners.comment: Yet to win a handicap but he's well drawn and won't mind the return to fast turf

runners.name: Habrdi
runners.spotlight: Hold-up handicapper who won a 7f Wolverhampton handicap off a 7lb lower mark last month; two creditable efforts have followed but he will need to raise his game to defy this mark and stall 1 will mean he needs some luck in running too.
runners.comment: AW win off 7lb lower last month; two fair runs since; will need luck from stall 1
runners.owner: Mrs S D Pearson And Mrs R Carr

runners.name: Crocodile Power
runners.spotlight: All four of his turf wins have come at Catterick but over 6f; creditable effort back from a break when third (5l behind Evocative Spark) two weeks ago; can build on that but perhaps vulnerable to stronger stayers.
runners.comment: Four-time course winner (6f, good or softer); promising return but this may not be his day

runners.name: Evocative Spark
runners.spotlight: His last two wins have come over C&D, the latest when powering past Vince Le Prince in the final furlong two weeks ago; a 4lb rise seems less of a concern than good to firm ground, a surface he is rarely seen on.
runners.comment: Ready C&D win two weeks ago (Vince Le Prince second); faster ground to deal with this time

runners.name: Roundhay Park
runners.spotlight: Course winner who scored at Ripon (6f, soft) last July and is just 2lb higher on this reappearance outing; however, 6f has been this 11yo's optimum trip in recent seasons, and this is his first run since October.
runners.comment: Veteran who returns from a 176-day absence over a trip that stretches him

runners.name: Jenni
runners.spotlight: Twice a winner in 7f turf handicaps; 3lb lower than for last May's Musselburgh win but that is a result of ending 2025 with a whimper; she has an absence to defy and others look safer.
runners.comment: On a good mark and conditions should be fine; ended 2025 quietly though

races.race_name: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Handicap

runners.name: Yorkshire Glory
runners.spotlight: Form took off this winter when dropped to 6f and his remarkable AW campaign, which saw him crowned All Weather Horse of the Year, featured nine wins between December and March; this track lends itself well to prominent racers but he anticipated the start and was slowly into his stide when finishing a well-beaten last back on turf at Pontefract yesterday; unraced on faster than good.
runners.comment: Remarkable AW campaign with nine wins December-March; last at Pontefract on Tuesday though

runners.name: No Return
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent for his current stable but of interest on his best effort, notably a second of eight at Lingfield (6f, AW) in January when 5lb higher; non-runner at Pontefract Tuesday.
runners.comment: Inconsistent but on a dangerous mark on this year's best; non-runner at Pontefract Tuesday

runners.name: Aberama Gold
runners.spotlight: Retains a large portion of his ability despite his advanced years and he is on a handy mark at present; no real excuses when fourth over C&D two weeks ago but this is a slightly weaker event.
runners.comment: On a good mark and he was a solid 4th over C&D two weeks ago; should be involved again

runners.name: Dorney Lake
runners.spotlight: Four 6f wins on good or faster turf; running himself into form on AW for his new yard this winter, most recently keeping on for third in a 0-80 at Southwell three weeks ago; 14lb lower than for his last turf outing; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Running himself back into form on AW; return to turf looks a major positive
runners.owner: Mrs C M Clarke, Foulrice Park Racing Ltd

runners.name: Rosenpur
runners.spotlight: Five wins, all around a left-hand bend (5f-5.5f; has form over much further), in a productive 2025 campaign; effective at this track and not impossibly treated returning from 170 days off; one to consider.
runners.comment: Five sprint wins around a left-hand bend last year; feasible mark for return; contender

runners.name: Vingegaard
runners.spotlight: Useful as 2yo for Archie Watson; absent for 501 days prior to three modest AW efforts for his new stable this year; dropped 11lb and now tried in blinkers so a revival isn't out of the question; not a solid option though.
runners.comment: Useful 2yo but yet to shine for new yard after a long absence; now tried in blinkers

runners.name: Free Pic
runners.spotlight: Won at Newcastle in October and solid fourth there (also 1m4f, AW) in February; didn't stay 2m back at that track last month but a return to 1m4f did the trick when taking a course handicap (good) a fortnight ago; big player if her stamina lasts out.
runners.comment: Won course handicap (1m4f, good) a fortnight ago; big player if her stamina holds out

runners.name: Stellarmasterpiece
runners.spotlight: Fine track record although her four wins have all come at 1m4f; both goes over C&D looked to just stretch her last year but she did plug on when fourth here (1m4f) a fortnight ago and can never be ruled out at this place.
runners.comment: Fine track record but all wins at 1m4f; respected despite some stamina doubts

runners.name: Regal Glory
runners.spotlight: 1-36 but it was a good effort in second when tackling this trip for the first time at Wolverhampton (AW) a fortnight ago; acts on turf and needs considering.
runners.comment: Good effort pver this trip when second (AW) last time; acts on turf too; respected

runners.name: Arctic Fox
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner at up to this trip on varying ground, including soft; back on the same mark she scored from at Carlisle (1m6f, soft) in June; chance rests on whether she's ready to roll for this reappearance.
runners.comment: Trip no bother and mark fine too; can have an impact if ready after 176 days away

runners.name: Nakatomi
runners.spotlight: 17-race maiden who's turned in a couple of fair efforts this year, including at this trip (AW) and when fifth to Free Pic here (1m4f, good) a fortnight ago; visor needs to eke out a little more, though.
runners.comment: 17-race maiden who's capable of a fair effort but the visor needs to eke out a little more
runners.owner: F Reay, S McMillan & S Pedersen

runners.name: Inlet
runners.spotlight: Has been hurdling for this new yard but yet to make an impact in that sphere; runner-up three times for Rae Guest (1m6f/2m, AW) but others hold greater interest on her return to this code.
runners.comment: Has been hurdling with little joy for this yard; others of more interest back on the Flat

runners.name: Rock Armour
runners.spotlight: In the hands of John Butler when sporting first-time blinkers to score at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) in February; that's her only success but a keeping-on third here (1m4f, good) was a respectable effort a fortnight ago and this new trip is probably worth a go.
runners.comment: Kept on for third here (1m4f) a fortnight ago; new trip probably worth a go

runners.name: Rajawail
runners.spotlight: Regressive maiden who is very difficult to fancy from out of the weights.
runners.comment: Regressive maiden who is very difficult to fancy from out of the weights

races.race_name: Powell & Construction Poultry Buildings Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Un Sens A La Vie
runners.spotlight: Record of 2-5 over hurdles and he was runner-up behind subsequent Supreme winner Old Park Star at Kempton (2m, good)  in November; finished a well-held fourth of 15 at Newbury in February but that was a major handicap and not many got involved in the testing conditions; sets a clear standard back in much calmer waters and his last win was over C&D; strong contender.
runners.comment: Dual hurdle winner and he sets a clear standard back in much calmer waters; strong claims

runners.name: Snatch A Glance
runners.spotlight: The last four of his six runs have been at Ludlow and he won a bumper in October and a maiden hurdle (2m, good) the following month; fell three out on his return from a break three weeks ago but he was travelling well enough in a prominent position at the time; this looks a tougher assignment but he still has potential and is respected for in-form yard.
runners.comment: Dual course winner who was prominent when he fell three out over C&D latest; respected

runners.name: Sonic Pioneer
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner whose form in juvenile hurdles includes an emphatic success at Kempton (2m, good) and two non-completions (pulled up both times) in Grade 2 races; drops back in class after a break but he has a bit to prove and probably needs to kick on again to make a major impact.
runners.comment: Drops back in class but he's been pulled up in his last two starts and has a bit to prove

runners.name: Not For Passin
runners.spotlight: Easy winner of a five-runner Perth bumper (good ground) last spring and later recruited for 60,000euros; fell when prominent in the market on hurdle/stable debut at Huntingdon in November and was well held in second when odds-on at Catterick last time; still early days in this sphere but he needs major improvement with equipment added after a break.
runners.comment: Still early days over hurdles but he needs major improvement after a break; equipment on

runners.name: Winston's Oath
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who showed ability in a Fakenham maiden (2m, good) two weeks ago but he has plenty to find here and is best watched.
runners.comment: Showed ability at Fakenham but he has a lot to find here and is best watched

runners.name: Latin
runners.spotlight: Six-time Flat winner including five on AW (1m-1m2f) since December; unexposed over hurdles and was fair fourth of 13 back in this sphere at Hereford (2m, good; 12-1) last week; should have more to offer as a jumper but this looks a tough task against Un Sens A La Vie.
runners.comment: Six-time Flat winner who is unexposed over hurdles but this looks a tough assignment

runners.name: Noonetellsmenothin
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 5yo who won a novice at Catterick (2m3f, good to soft) in December; finished lame when pulled up on handicap debut at Kelso (2m7f, heavy) in February and he bounced back with a fair fourth at Market Rasen (2m4f; cheekpieces added) last month; looks worth another try at this sort of trip and he's not ruled out in first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Unexposed 5yo and he could go well back up in trip; first-time blinkers

runners.name: Tzarmix
runners.spotlight: Back from a break with a clearcut win over C&D (good to soft) in December and he's finished runner-up in three subsequent starts; up another 2lb but he had a near-miss at Market Rasen (2m7f) last time and this return to quicker ground is no problem (won a point on good to firm); respected.
runners.comment: C&D win in December and he had a near-miss at Market Rasen last time; respected

runners.name: Maximum Offers
runners.spotlight: 2m hurdle winner who landed the odds with an easy win in a handicap chase at Hereford (3m1f, good) last Thursday; that was a three-runner race but he won by a long way and is still unexposed at staying trips; major player again off 5lb higher back over hurdles.
runners.comment: Easy win over fences (3m1f) last week; major player again off 5lb higher back over hurdles
runners.owner: L H & A Snowden & J E Snowden

runners.name: Catwalk Girl
runners.spotlight: Point winner who has shown some promise in three hurdle runs this year, including when third of five at Wincanton (2m5f, good) three weeks ago; should have more to offer now switched to a handicap and her dam was a 3m winner over fences; one to keep an eye on.
runners.comment: Handicap newcomer and she needs watching in market at this new trip
runners.owner: The Galloping Dentist At 45 The Green

runners.name: Springs A Girl
runners.spotlight: Won a novice here (2m5f, good to soft) in December 2024 but she's been up and down in handicaps since; finished second of five at Newbury (3m, soft) in February but she didn't fire at Chepstow (2m3f) last time; now goes back up in trip and down in grade but this is competitive and others are more persuasive.
runners.comment: On workable mark but she's been up and down in handicaps and others are more persuasive

runners.name: The Egyptian Ginge
runners.spotlight: 0-9 but was runner-up on handicap debut at Haydock (2m3f, good to soft) last month and he filled the same position behind an odds-on rival in a maiden at Ascot (2m3f, good) last time; needs to find more at this new trip but he still has potential and is a half-brother to a 2m7f chase winner.
runners.comment: Runner-up in his only handicap and he's in the mix at this new trip

runners.name: Windsor Blue
runners.spotlight: Off the mark with 20-1 win in a handicap here (2m5f, soft) in February but she failed to back that up on her return visit last month; still has low mileage but she needs to resume her progress at this new trip.
runners.comment: Won here (2m5f) in February but she failed to back that up on her return visit last month

races.race_name: Hr Smith Handicap Chase (For The Boyne Cup) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Petty Cash
runners.spotlight: Won over fences at Chepstow (2m7f, good to soft) in November 2024 but he's not seen much action since; ran well after wind surgery when third over C&D in February but he was pulled up at Newbury (2m6f) last month; tongue-tie is now added and he needs to bounce back again after that last run.
runners.comment: Has not seen much action in recent years and was pulled at Newbury (2m6f) last month

runners.name: Annie Express
runners.spotlight: Has a patchy profile but she made it 2-6 over fences when justifying favouritism here (2m4f, good) last month; up 6lb but that was a personal best last time and her other chase win came over this C&D; key player.
runners.comment: C&D winner who scored in good style over 2m4f here last time; big player again up 6lb

runners.name: Gris Majeur
runners.spotlight: Triple hurdle winner who made the breakthrough over fences in a Ludlow handicap (2m4f, good to firm) in October; that was a four-runner race but he's been placed in four runs since including when runner-up at Chepstow (2m7f) 11 days ago; should go well again and all of his wins have been on good/good to firm.
runners.comment: In-form 9yo who was runner-up off this mark at Chepstow (2m7f) 11 days ago; respected
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Brian Davies

runners.name: Tax For Max
runners.spotlight: Three wins at around 2m in Ireland and latest was over fences for Willie Mullins in June 2023; record of 0-8 for current yard but he rallied well when a creditable third here (2m, good) last month; this is a different test at this new trip but he's well treated on his old form and needs watching in the market; tongue-tie back on.
runners.comment: Creditable third over 2m here last month; this is a different test but he's not ruled out

runners.name: Almazhar Garde
runners.spotlight: Not the force of old and has failed to complete in his last two starts but this is a drop back in grade and he went close at Catterick (3m1f, good) in November; looks interesting on that form and he made all when winning this race last year.
runners.comment: Failed to complete last two starts but on a dangerous mark and he won this race last year

runners.name: Zacony Rebel
runners.spotlight: Won a five-runner handicap at Huntingdon (2m7f, soft) in December but this veteran has struggled in five subsequent runs; has lots to prove and he's a long way out of the weights.
runners.comment: Won in December but he's struggled since and is a long way out of the weights here

runners.name: Epic West
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner who was 2-3 as a novice hurdler last season (wins gained on soft/good to soft); his three runs over fences since December include a close second at Warwick (2m, good to soft) but he was tailed off in both runs either side of that; still lightly raced but he has something to prove on this big step back up in trip.
runners.comment: 2-3 over hurdles but he's been tailed off in two of his three chase runs; risks attached

runners.name: Arrycan
runners.spotlight: 12yo who has won four times over fences but there were more bad runs than good ones in Ireland last year; on workable mark in suitable conditions but he was tailed off when last seen in October and needs a major revival for new yard.
runners.comment: On workable mark in suitable conditions but he needs to bounce back on return for new yard

races.race_name: Powell & Company Construction Stables & Buildings Mares' Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Klervia
runners.spotlight: Overall record of 0-10 but she was a respectable fourth of 13 in Exeter handicap (2m5f, good; cheekpieces added) two weeks ago; continues to drop down the weights and has each-way claims if she can build on that last run.
runners.comment: Overall record of 0-10 but was fair fourth of 13 at Exeter (2m5f, good) latest; in the mix

runners.name: Shes All Pride
runners.spotlight: Improved form upped to 2m6f on handicap debut when third from off the pace at Stratford (good; lost shoe) last month; 3lb out of the weights again but she was only beaten around 2l last time and that was behind a progressive rival; dangerous.
runners.comment: Improved form when third at Stratford; 3lb out of the weights again but she's in this mix

runners.name: After Many Days
runners.spotlight: 0-18 but she's finished runner-up in her last three handicaps and latest was at Hereford (2m4f, good; 15-8 favourite) last month; up another 2lb but she still looks feasibly treated and should be in the thick of things again.
runners.comment: 0-18 but she's finished runner-up at around 2m4f in her last three handicaps; respected

runners.name: Jlow
runners.spotlight: Longstanding maiden who made it 0-11 over hurdles when a well-held fourth in Ffos Las handicap (2m4f, good to soft; 25-1) ten days ago; others preferred.
runners.comment: 0-11 over hurdles and was a well-held fourth at Ffos Las ten days ago; opposable

runners.name: Kottayam
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden who was pulled up in two handicaps (2m3f/3m1f) for Dylan Cunha in October; had wind surgery since but she's with another new yard now and needs a major revival after six months off.
runners.comment: 0-7 and pulled up in both handicaps; had wind op and returns for another new yard

runners.name: Lady Dapple Rose
runners.spotlight: Second of four on hurdling debut at Leicester (1m7f, soft) in November but she's not gone on from that and was a remote third in a Chepstow maiden last month; now switches to a handicap needing improvement on this step up in trip.
runners.comment: Unexposed 5yo but he needs improvement upped in trip on handicap debut

runners.name: Slack Alice
runners.spotlight: 14-race maiden on Flat and over jumps; well beaten back over hurdles in her last three starts including in a handicap at Newcastle (2m4f, good to soft) three weeks ago; still unexposed in this sphere but she needs a transformation back on good ground; visor reapplied.
runners.comment: Still unexposed over hurdles but she needs a transformation back on good ground

runners.name: Jessie's Giant
runners.spotlight: Down the field in two bumpers and she's finished tailed off at big prices in three hurdle runs (2m); now goes handicapping but she's out of the weights and needs major improvement on this big step up in trip.
runners.comment: Handicap debutante but she's out of the weights and needs major improvement upped in trip

runners.name: La Cantate
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 5yo but she hasn't progressed over hurdles so far and has been well held in two handicaps (2m4f/2m2f) for her current yard; returns after 276 days off and she needs improvement back up in trip.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 5yo but she hasn't progressed over hurdles so far; opposable on her return

runners.name: Wishful Wings
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 5yo who hasn't finished closer than 12l to a winner and didn't get very far over fences here (unseated at third) last month; still unexposed in handicaps but cheekpieces need to make a difference back over hurdles.
runners.comment: Still unexposed in handicaps but cheekpieces need to make a difference back over hurdles

runners.name: Imperial Pride
runners.spotlight: Veteran who has turned things around over fences recently, with a win at Huntingdon (2m7f, good to soft) followed by a second on similar ground at Plumpton (3m1f); now returns to hurdling but she's lightly raced in this sphere and looks interesting off the same mark as last time.
runners.comment: Win and second over fences on last two starts and she's respected back over hurdles

races.race_name: Powell & Company Construction Agricultural Buildings Mares' Open Maiden NH Flat Race (C3 Elim) (GBB)

runners.name: Milly Ocean
runners.spotlight: Attracted support into 14-1 and she showed clear promise when fourth of 14 on Huntingdon debut (2m, good) last month; just about sets the standard on that form and she should be more streetwise this time; key player for yard with record of 2-3 in bumpers here this season.
runners.comment: Promising fourth at Huntingdon and just about sets the standard on that form; key player

runners.name: Chocolate Cosmos
runners.spotlight: Well-related filly; ran well for a long way when fifth over C&D (33-1) in January but she failed to build on that when ninth of 14 at Huntingdon last month; enters the reckoning on her initial form but has something to prove after that last run.
runners.comment: Showed ability on C&D debut but she failed to build on that at Huntingdon last month

runners.name: Britway Belle
runners.spotlight: Bought for £120,000 after winning an Irish mares' maiden point (3m, yielding) on debut in October; tailed off on bumper debut at Hereford in January but she showed promise with her third at that track (2m, good) last month; has possibilities if she can build on that and she's a half-sister to a bumper winner.
runners.comment: Irish point winner who was a promising third in a Hereford bumper last month; in the mix

runners.name: Thatsnotmyname
runners.spotlight: £4,000 yearling, £2,000 3yo; tenth foal; half-sister to winners San Rumoldo (bumper/2m4f hurdle), En Joule (bumper) and Perculator (bumper and 2m5f/3m hurdle); dam unraced sister to high-class bumper and 2m1f-3m chase winner The Tother One; yard has had a debut bumper winner this year and she needs watching in the market.
runners.comment: Yard has had a debut bumper winner this year and she needs watching in market

runners.name: Meliden Mountain
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam unraced sister to useful 2m/2m1f hurdle winner Hearts Are Trumps, out of unraced sister to Cheltenham Gold Cup winner Denman; yard 15% in bumpers this season and she's an interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: Out of unraced sister to a useful hurdler; interesting newcomer who needs a close look

runners.name: Oakstead Park
runners.spotlight: 35,000euros 3yo; twelfth foal; sister to bumper/2m5f hurdle winner Lime Avenue, half-sister to three winners including Rogue Angel (smart 2m-3m5f hurdle/chase including Irish Grand National) and Fire In His Eyes (useful bumper/2m4f hurdle); dam unraced; one of two runners for the yard and interesting to see how she figures in the market.
runners.comment: 35,000euros 3yo; one of two for the yard and interesting to see how she figures in market

runners.name: Belle Justice
runners.spotlight: £4,500 2yo, £15,000 3yo; closely related to Czech 1m6f/2m winner Concord Of Gracie, half-sister to five winners including Cool Macavity (7f-1m2f Flat, RPR 91; useful 2m hurdle) and Easy Peasy (bumper/2m hurdle); dam 1m2f/1m3f winner (81); yard 1-14 in bumpers this season and market should guide on debut.
runners.comment: Yard 1-14 in bumpers this season and market should guide on debut

runners.name: Lightning Luna
runners.spotlight: Well-held fourth of six in a British point last month and is probably best watched on her rules debut.
runners.comment: Well-held fourth of six in a British point last month; best watched on her rules debut

runners.name: Arm's Of An Angel
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in her first two Irish points before being carried out by a loose horse in her final run in November; can only be watched now switched to rules for new yard.
runners.comment: Failed to complete in three Irish points and can only be watched on her hurdling debut

runners.name: Mrs Cravatte
runners.spotlight: Struggled in her first two points in May 2024 and she fell on her comeback at Siddington (3m, good) last month; opposable on rules debut.
runners.comment: Didn't show much in three British points and she's best watched on rules debut

runners.name: Whiting Ash
runners.spotlight: Dam triple point winner, from family of smart staying chasers Over The Deel and Smarty; not an obvious type on pedigree and she could be a longer-term prospect.
runners.comment: Not an obvious type on pedigree and she could be a longer-term prospect

runners.name: Rose Of Knocknamoe
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in an Irish point in December but she was beaten 13l and only three completed; stumbled after getting hampered on recent rules debut at Stratford (2m, good; 9-1), finishing a well-held sixth, and needs major improvement here; stablemate of Milly Ocean.
runners.comment: Well-held second in sole Irish point and was down the field on rules debut at Stratford

runners.name: Donwell Abbey
runners.spotlight: Has a striking pedigree but she made a low-key start when 12th of 14 at Huntingdon (good; 18-1) last month, running green; needs to leave that form a long way behind.
runners.comment: Made a low-key start at Huntingdon and she needs to leave that form well behind

runners.name: Mysti Meadow
runners.spotlight: 14-1 and raced alone on the inner in the home straight when fifth of 14 on last month's debut over C&D (good to soft); that was an encouraging start from this well-related 5yo and she should benefit from that experience; in the mix.
runners.comment: Well-related 5yo who made an encouraging start over C&D last month; in the mix

races.race_name: Eddie Mapp Memorial Open Hunters' Chase

runners.name: Mr Glass
runners.spotlight: Won races for Paul Nicholls and Will Biddick and his last success was in a hunter chase at Wincanton (3m1f, heavy) last January; that form puts him in the picture and he made a respectable stable debut when third at Exeter (3m, good to soft) last month; possibilities.
runners.comment: Fair third on stable debut at Exeter and has possibilities if he can build on that

runners.name: Potters Party
runners.spotlight: Fairly useful chase form for Henry de Bromhead as recently as winter 2024-25; unseated on hunter chase debut at Hexham last Monday but that was after two solid efforts in points and he should have a future in this sphere; respected.
runners.comment: Fairly useful chaser in Ireland and he should have a future in hunter chases; respected

runners.name: Prohus Yank
runners.spotlight: Dual point winner but he was well held in that sphere two weeks ago; failed to complete in two previous runs over regulation fences but he held a narrow lead when he unseated at the last over C&D (soft) in February, and that race was won by subsequent Cheltenham and Aintree hero Barton Snow; looks interesting on that form and he's a key player back in a hunter chase.
runners.comment: Ties in with Barton Snow on some C&D form in February and he's a key player

runners.name: Fix At All
runners.spotlight: Back to form with some respectable efforts this spring but his second at Kempton (2m4f, good) was in a match and he was well held when third here last time; record for current yard is now 0-7 and he looks vulnerable again back up in trip.
runners.comment: Record of 0-8 for current yard and he looks vulnerable again back up in trip

runners.name: The Paddy Pie
runners.spotlight: Eight-time chase winner at up 3m1f but he's well into the veteran stage now and has struggled in three points this year; this looks tough on these terms back over regulation fences and others are preferred.
runners.comment: 13yo who has struggled in three points this year; best watched back over regulation fences

runners.name: Est Illic
runners.spotlight: Won a small-field point recently but this veteran is a modest performer under rules these days and was pulled up in two handicaps (including over C&D) in February; opposable back in this sphere.
runners.comment: Won a point recently but he's a modest performer under rules these days; opposable

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Maiden

runners.name: Perisher
runners.spotlight: Promise in three maidens last year on varying ground, including when third at Listowel on heavy (beaten 8l into third by Straight Up); ran well on handicap debut 18 days ago when 2.25l third to Empty Wallet, first run since being gelded and in first-time cheekpieces; should be thereabouts for in-form stable.
runners.comment: Good third on h'cap debut 18 days ago, first run since being gelded; should be thereabouts

runners.name: Sanctijude
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden but some good efforts and placed four times, including over course and distance in October when beaten a nose by Marjorie Dew; good return to action when beaten 2.5l into fourth by Drop Dead Gorgeous at Naas on soft 31 days ago, awkward away; consistent, has experience edge and obvious player.
runners.comment: Seven-race maiden but consistent; C&D form, second in Oct; good return at Naas; big player

runners.name: Jojo's Legacy
runners.spotlight: Three promising runs last year on yielding to good, beaten 3.5l into fourth behind dual Group 1 winner Precise at Cork in September over 1m; below that when beaten 7.5l into eighth on AW 47 days ago; chance if coming on for that; stable in form.
runners.comment: Good form in three turf runs in 2025; below that AW 47 days ago; chance if bouncing back

runners.name: Keogie
runners.spotlight: 21,000euros yearling; half-brother to Czech 7f 2yo/1m3f winner Riwetta; promising debut when 2.5l second at the Curragh 38 days ago on heavy, staying on well to come clear of remainder; step up in trip should suit; chance with more to come.
runners.comment: Promising debut when 2.5l second at the Curragh 38 days ago; up in trip will suit; chance

runners.name: Casla's Eagle
runners.spotlight: Gleneagles filly; dam 1m winner (RPR 77), half-sister to winners Square Dance (7f 2yo/1m), Concessions (7f 2yo) and Balcony (1m), out of smart 1m-1m4f winner; makes debut for good yard that doesn't have many 3yo runners on the Flat; market can guide.
runners.comment: Gleneagles filly; dam 1m winner (RPR 77); half-sister to three winners (7f-1m); debut

runners.name: Alphonsus Liguori
runners.spotlight: Cost 210,000gns as a yearling; Study Of Man colt; half-brother to winners Galmarley (1m5f; RPR 101), Gallifrey (1m 2yo/12.4f AW; 88) and Corundum (1m AW; 76); dam 7f (2yo) and 1m (Listed) winner (103), out of unraced half-sister to Derby runner-up Walk In The Park; interesting debutant for trainer in form; one of two for stable.
runners.comment: 210,000gns Y ; half-brother to three winners (1m-1m5f); dam 7f 2yo winner; trainer in form

runners.name: Cosmic Funk
runners.spotlight: Price rose from 34,000euros to 38,000euros from foal to yearling; first foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Majestic Myles (7f Listed), Parbold (Group-placed 6f 2yo) and Majestic Moon (useful 6f/7f); top yard; market can guide but tricky draw to overcome.
runners.comment: 38,000euros Y; first foal of unraced dam, half-sister to three winners (6f-7f, Listed)

runners.name: Ocean Echo
runners.spotlight: Awtaad gelding; fourth foal; half-brother to winners Arabian Story (1m inc AW; RPR 105) and Olympic Candle (6f-1m inc 2yo/AW; 93); dam French 6f-8.5f maiden (73), half-sister to 1m Group 1 winner Territories, out of useful 1m/1m1f 2yo winner; well-bred type been gelded before debut; market can guide.
runners.comment: Awtaad gelding; half-brother to two winners (6f-1m); dam half-sister to G1 winner; debut
runners.owner: R Scahill, S Begley, J & C Bourke

runners.name: Lord Aus
runners.spotlight: Looked badly in need of the experience when beaten 12.5l into eighth on debut at Navan over 1m2f 23 days ago; should progress but plenty of stamina in pedigree and drop in trip not sure to suit.
runners.comment: Needed experience when eighth on debut 23 days ago; will progress; drop in trip not ideal

runners.name: Tavarua
runners.spotlight: Showed something to work on but well-held when sixth on debut at this track 14 days ago; needs plenty more.
runners.comment: Well-held when sixth on debut at this track 14 days ago; needs plenty more

runners.name: Rapide Vega
runners.spotlight: Improved plenty for first run and a break last year when stepped up to 7f in Galway maiden in October, beaten 5.5l into fourth; third has won since; off 177 days and type to progress as 3yo; up in trip.
runners.comment: Improved plenty to be fourth in Galway maiden (7f); up in trip; progression likely

runners.name: Priorstown
runners.spotlight: Showed ability when beaten 11l into sixth on debut at Navan (1m, heavy) 23 days ago; should come on for that but may need further to show best.
runners.comment: Showed ability on debut when sixth at Navan 23 days ago; will improve but may need further

runners.name: Meriden
runners.spotlight: Not shown enough in two starts this year over 6f on soft ground at the Curragh and on the AW to suggest she's ready to win a contest of this nature; more of a long-term project; up in trip.
runners.comment: Well held in two maidens this year on soft and the AW; more of a long-term project

runners.name: Borora Aura
runners.spotlight: Well beaten seventh at Bellewstown 11 days ago on debut; wears hood; needs significant improvement to figure.
runners.comment: Well beaten seventh at Bellewstown 11 days ago on debut; needs big improvement to figure

runners.name: Sands Castle
runners.spotlight: Didn't beat many rivals in two starts last year; up against it.
runners.comment: Didn't beat many rivals in two starts last year; up against it.

runners.name: Sindagan
runners.spotlight: Shaped with lots of promise in two hot maidens last season, finishing third and fourth at Naas and the Curragh; first run since being gelded on return 31 days ago, beaten 2.5l by Accredited on heavy; third has won since so form likely strong; handles soft and big danger to Go Just Do It; first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Plenty of promise in three strong-looking maidens; handles soft; first-time cp; big chance
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Go Just Do It
runners.spotlight: Ran to a very useful level in three starts last season on varying ground from 6f-7f; beaten 1l on debut behind New Zealand at the Curragh; improved to be beaten 5.5l into third in Group 2 by the smart Constitution River; similar level when second to Trojan Warrior dropped in trip 183 days ago, went with enthusiasm and traded short in-running; handles soft and should be tough to beat on return for yard in form.
runners.comment: Ran to a very useful level in three starts, inc in G2 company; handles soft; tough to beat

runners.name: Chestnut Palace
runners.spotlight: Stayed on well to finish fifth in Naas maiden on debut in March; bit below that up in trip at this track 13 days ago but only beaten 7l when last of seven; better effort incoming down in trip but likely not enough.
runners.comment: Bit below debut form when beaten 7l last of seven lto; drop in trip should suit

runners.name: Solana Beach
runners.spotlight: No Nay Never filly; half-sister to winners Dandhu (7f inc AW 2yo/turf Listed; RPR 107), Gustavus Vassa (1m2f; 102), Dutch Courage (6f/7f 2yo; 98) and Maid A Million (6f/7f inc AW 2yo; 97); dam unraced half-sister to 1m Group 1 winner Rajeem; yard does well with 2yos and trainer won this last year; worth a market check.
runners.comment: No Nay Never filly, half-sister to four winners (6f-1m2f, inc Listed); yard good with 2yos

runners.name: Squishy
runners.spotlight: Beaten just the one rival in two starts this term; hard to make a case for.
runners.comment: Beaten just the one rival in two starts this term; hard to make a case for

runners.name: In The Gloaming
runners.spotlight: 33,000euros yearling; half-brother to two winners (5f to 6f, inc Group-placed); last of 12 on debut at the Curragh on debut 206 days ago; probably capable of better and gelded since but huge ask here.
runners.comment: Last of 12 on debut at the Curragh on debut 206 days ago; gelded since; huge ask
runners.owner: Team Valor International LLC & A Oliver

runners.name: Jp's Express
runners.spotlight: Decent efforts when midfield in a pair of maidens last year on heavy ground at Galway and on the AW; far more needed here, however.
runners.comment: Decent efforts when midfield in a pair of maidens (soft and AW); far more needed here

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap

runners.name: Pink Oxalis
runners.spotlight: Four wins last year, including over a mile at this track on soft in April; won very competitive fillies handicap at Leopardstown in September off 5lb lower; not been in same form in two starts this term and needs a big step forward down in trip off top weight.
runners.comment: Course winner; big improver last year; not in same form two starts this term; top weight

runners.name: Independent Expert
runners.spotlight: Eight-time winner, five over this trip; landed 7f handicap at Naas on soft two starts ago; ran well off 4lb higher when beaten 6l into sixth at the Curragh 19 days ago; should go well back in trip and has won off higher in the past.
runners.comment: Ran well off revised mark when beaten 6l at the Curragh lto; down in trip; each-way chance

runners.name: Gloriously Glam
runners.spotlight: Won twice on turf last season, including at this track over a mile on good; last of 16 at the Curragh on return 19 days ago and doesn't want testing ground so up against it.
runners.comment: Course winner; last of 16 on return and doesn't want testing ground; up against it
runners.owner: Dooley Thoroughbreds & J Fairhurst & Deepak Dhand

runners.name: Greydreambeliever
runners.spotlight: Some useful form in Britain before being bought by current connections for 30,000gns; ran a very encouraging race on stable debut when beaten 4.25l by stablemate Sindria at this track on soft over a mile 14 days ago; nudged up 3lb; drop in trip should suit and one of three strong chances for in-form stable with great record in this race.
runners.comment: Encouraging stable debut when second here over 1m 14 days ago; drop in trip will suit
runners.owner: K Blake/Mrs E Palmer/M Wilson/N Luck

runners.name: Glory To Be
runners.spotlight: Navan maiden winner over 5f on soft in October; returned this term in fine form, runner-up at the Curragh over 6f before going one better at Leopardstown over 7f (yielding) seven days ago; unexposed over this trip and definite player off 6lb higher.
runners.comment: Improved for step up in trip when landing h'cap lto at Leopardstown; player off 6lb higher

runners.name: Greek Flower
runners.spotlight: On a 16-race losing streak but shaped well on a number of occasions, including on return 7 days ago when beaten 1.75l by Glory To Be at Leopardstown on yielding; has slipped to an attractive mark and a leading player if building on return.
runners.comment: Long losing streak but shaped well on return, third behind Glory To Be; on a good mark

runners.name: Rion Rubette
runners.spotlight: Bolted up by 8l at Limerick over 7f in June; handicapper hammered her with 18lb after and that has proven too burdensome since; did shape well [not beaten far behind Glory To Be in second) on return when beaten 4.25l at the Curragh 19 days ago but testing ground wouldn't be ideal back up in trip.
runners.comment: Mark suffered after bolting up at Limerick seven starts ago; would prefer good ground

runners.name: Alibah
runners.spotlight: Course and distance maiden winner on debut last year; ran big race when beaten under a length in Listed company at Fairyhouse afterwards; below that form when well-held in Group 3 206 days ago but that may have come too soon; stiff opening mark but open to improvement; one of three for yard that have won four out of the last five renewals.
runners.comment: C&D maiden winner, ran big race when third in Listed race nto; below form lto but excuses

runners.name: Tahcawin
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at fourth attempt over 6f on yielding in Fairyhouse nursery in September, making light work of opening mark of 70 in beating Zarvali by 4.25l; ran well six days later when beaten 9l into 12th by Dorset in Goffs Millions; off 207 days; trainer has good record at this course and she's the type to progress but bad draw.
runners.comment: Made light work of opening mark of 70 in Fairyhouse nursery; should progress but bad draw

runners.name: Zous And Me
runners.spotlight: Knew enough first time out to make a winning debut at Galway in September, beating Sanctijude by 0.5l over 7f on soft; never landed a blow in Group 3 company afterwards but very unexposed and opening mark doesn't look harsh; leading player for top course trainer.
runners.comment: Galway maiden winner; found G3 company too hot after; unexposed; opening mark looks okay

runners.name: Varshini
runners.spotlight: Course and distance maiden winner on this card last year; improved plenty for seasonal return when beaten 0.5l into third in Curragh handicap 19 days ago, hitting the line much better; up 2lb but still well-treated back up in trip and enjoys soft ground.
runners.comment: C&D maiden winner; improved for return to be beaten 0.5l lto; up 2lb but well-treated

runners.name: Lord Massusus
runners.spotlight: Won this in 2023; consistent stakes performer with seven wins; won Heritage Stakes last season, beating Mutasarref by a neck; best subsequent effort came when fourth in Minstrel Stakes in July; two starts this season, improved from return to be beaten 3.25l behind The Lion In Winter at Leopardstown seven days ago; down in trip; mightn't want the ground too testing but shouldn't be far away and trainer has good course record.
runners.comment: Seven-time winner; ran to rating in Listed race seven days ago; shouldn't be far away

runners.name: Saracen
runners.spotlight: Impressive maiden winner at the Curragh in 2024; promising third in Greenham Stakes last year but finished down the field in Jersey Stakes; disappointed when last of ten in Gladness on return 25 days ago; needs to come on plenty for that but still lightly-raced and handles soft ground.
runners.comment: Promising third in Greenham last year; well beat on return at Curragh; could bounce back

runners.name: City Of Memphis
runners.spotlight: Looked an exciting prospect when bolting up in Cork maiden in May, beating Bonus Time by 2.75l in good style; thrown into Irish 1,000 Guineas after and acquitted herself very well, beaten 4.5l by Lake Victoria into fifth, despite racing keenly; off 332 days; trip will suit; very unexposed and big chance, despite a less than ideal draw.
runners.comment: Cork maiden winner, ran very good fifth in Irish 1,000 Guineas 332 days ago; big chance

runners.name: Spicy Margarita
