runners.spotlight: Sole win in 15 starts came in 7f AW maiden February of last year; highly-tried since; best effort came when beaten 2l into fourth by One Look in Park Express 12 months ago; well beat into eighth in same race 19 days ago; ground unlikely to suit and up against it.
runners.comment: Highly-tried last year, couple of good efforts in Group races; ground likely too soft here
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Tuscan Hills
runners.spotlight: Listed winner for Raphael Freire over 1m at Pontefract (soft) in 2024; never landed a blow in three starts last year, including in Derby; down the field on stable debut in Lincoln 38 days ago; needs plenty more.
runners.comment: Listed winner in 2024; no impact in good company last year; down field on stable debut lto

runners.name: Cowardofthecounty
runners.spotlight: Group 3-winning two-year-old in 2024; struggled in three runs last year in stakes and handicap company; showed more and ran well for a long way from the front in Listed Heritage Stakes 7 days ago, eventually finishing eighth behind The Lion In Winter; should come on for that and step back in trip will suit; one of two for in-form yard.
runners.comment: Group 3 winner who struggled last season; ran well in Listed lto; drop in trip will suit

runners.name: Tina's Indian
runners.spotlight: Impressive Curragh maiden winner in August 2024; below that form early last season but finished well with a pair of third-placed finishes at the Curragh and Naas in competitive handicaps (soft and yielding) before winning conditions race on last start, beating Senna's Girl by a neck as 1-2 favourite; form of that questionable and he's not well treated at the weights here.
runners.comment: Curragh maiden winner; won conditions race 171 days ago; handles soft, hard ask at weights
runners.owner: C F Harrington & James John Hughes

races.race_name: Dining Packages At Gowran Park Handicap

runners.name: Bullet Bourbon
runners.spotlight: Much improved when second at 50-1 in a 6f handicap at Dundalk three weeks ago on seasonal debut; encouraging that her best run at two was in a C&D nursery on heavy ground.
runners.comment: Much improved over 6f at Dundalk on return, best run at two was over C&D on heavy ground
runners.owner: David Daly & Gerard Shevlin & D Battersby

runners.name: Survivor's Code
runners.spotlight: Two 7f nursery outings were respectable; appeared not to stay 1m2f on reappearance/stable debut at Navan; got the better of a battle with Monocle Rocket over C&D a fortnight ago; 5lb higher now, the runner-up may turn the tables.
runners.comment: Got the better of a battle with Monocle Rocket here a fortnight ago, 5lb higher now

runners.name: Unfamiliar
runners.spotlight: Three unplaced runs at Dundalk; stayed on in mid-field over 6f on handicap/turf debut at Naas; failed to run to expectations when 9-2 chance for recent C&D race won by Survivor's Code.
runners.comment: Failed to run to expectations when 9-2 chance for recent C&D race won by Survivor's Code

runners.name: Monocle Rocket
runners.spotlight: Ran three times at two, twice finished last; not a bad run on middle start; made a promising start for this stable when making most of the running over C&D two weeks ago; fair chance of reversing form with the more exposed winner Survivor's Code.
runners.comment: Bright start for this stable over C&D, good chance of reversing form with Survivor's Code

runners.name: Imnotleavinyou
runners.spotlight: Best run last season when third on nursery debut over 5f at Naas in September; failed to build on that on her last three outings of the campaign; much more like it when runner-up from 4lb out of the handicap at Bellewstown on seasonal debut; may reverse form with the winner Dream Legend,
runners.comment: Runner-up when out of the handicap at Bellewstown, may reverse form with Dream Legend

runners.name: Windsor Lily
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far in a 6f maiden on second Dundalk start; never showed over C&D a fortnight ago; chance is not obvious.
runners.comment: Fair run on second AW start, never showed here a fortnight ago, chance is not obvious
runners.owner: Shane R Stafford & Crampscastle Bloodstock Ltd

runners.name: Spirit Above
runners.spotlight: Misfired gamble in a maiden at Dundalk in February; huge price at Dundalk three weeks ago on handicap debut and ran accordingly.
runners.comment: Misfired gamble at Dundalk in February, huge price on handicap debut and ran accordingly

runners.name: Dream Legend
runners.spotlight: Down the field in turf/AW maidens last year; best handicap effort in November when not beaten far in sixth at Dundalk (6f); long-priced Bellewstown winner recently on first attempt at 1m, took the lead at today's distance; not guaranteed to uphold form with Imnotleavinyou.
runners.comment: Long-priced Bellewstown winner on first attempt at 1m, took the lead at today's distance
runners.owner: E D Delany & Mrs Lisa Delany

runners.name: Chirac
runners.spotlight: A glimmer of promise on the second of three AW runs; took a step in the right direction with fourth at Naas over 5f on turf/handicap debut; form has been boosted by the winner Oh Cecilia and third-placed Bay Of Supremacy.
runners.comment: Took a step in the right direction with fourth over 5f on turf/handicap debut, solid form

runners.name: Glen Breeze
runners.spotlight: Appeared to find 5f inadequate at Bellewstown in his first handicap; can probably do better at this trip; others have stronger credentials.
runners.comment: Appeared to find 5f inadequate in his first handicap, can probably do better at this trip

runners.name: Honey Dale
runners.spotlight: Needs to improve considerably on Dundalk form to make any impact here; the addition of cheekpieces failed to do the trick last time, discarded now.
runners.comment: Needs to improve considerably on Dundalk form to make any impact here

runners.name: Nephin Mountain
runners.spotlight: Leopardstown run last week may have come too soon after C&D fourth behind Survivor's Code; may struggle to reverse the form of that race with Monocle Rocket.
runners.comment: Leopardstown run last week may have come too soon after C&D fourth behind Survivor's Code

runners.name: Moonlight Molly
runners.spotlight: Out of her depth in maidens last year; mid-field at Leopardstown ten days ago on handicap debut; needs to find extra.
runners.comment: Mid-field at Leopardstown ten days ago on handicap debut, needs to find extra

runners.name: Manhattan Dandy
runners.spotlight: Three-time turf winner and often placed but 0-22 on AW and placed only three times; hard to make a strong case on recent AW form; however, he is so much more effective on turf that a good showing is not out of the question.
runners.comment: Hard to make a strong case on recent AW form, more effective on turf, not ruled out

runners.name: Zabriskie Point
runners.spotlight: Both wins came as a juvenile for Charlie Hills at 5f/7f on different ground types; has plunged in the ratings and profited from a drop to this grade when third at Leopardstown last week; likely contender with Reese Holohan's useful 7lb a help in easing his burden.
runners.comment: Profited from a drop to this grade when third at Leopardstown last week, likely contender
runners.owner: B White & M W White & Ms H McGuinness & D Sheridan

runners.name: Half Nutz
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner but his last success was in September 2022; no sign of the drought ending; down the field in two sprint handicap this season.
runners.comment: Six-time winner but his last success was in September 2022, no sign of the drought ending

runners.name: Kitty Bear
runners.spotlight: Breakthrough win came at the 17th attempt in a 1m Dundalk handicap in January under this rider; strong in the market earlier this month when beaten a short head into second on AW (1m); ran a little below market expectations at Leopardstown last week; Zabriskie Point has her measure on that running
runners.comment: Goes well for this rider, stable in fine form, ran a little below expectations last week
runners.owner: Charles Kerwin & Brendan Kerwin & Liam Kehoe

runners.name: Miss Americana
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability at a modest level last season; went close over 1m here on seasonal debut with Hell Left Loose 1/2l away in third; Leopardstown run only three days later may have come too soon for her.
runners.comment: Went close over 1m here on seasonal debut, Leopardstown run may have come too soon for her

runners.name: Rock Etoile
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner; out of form at Dundalk during the winter; in rear here 13 days ago in a race in which Miss Americana and Hell Let Loose were placed.
runners.comment: Six-time winner; out of form at Dundalk during the winter, in rear here 13 days ago
runners.owner: Conor McKenna & Patrick J McKenna & Emmet McKenna

runners.name: Majestic King
runners.spotlight: E320,000 yearling and 3,000gns Godolphin cast-off; well-related; down the field on only start last season; had plenty of horses behind in a 1m Curragh maiden on reappearance; failed to improve at Navan; handicap debut now; worth a check to see how the market rates his chance relative to stablemate Hell Let Loose.
runners.comment: Worth a check to see how the market rates his chance relative to stablemate Hell Let Loose

runners.name: Secret Magician
runners.spotlight: Only three wins from 49 outings; placed seven times but has failed to make the frame in six starts since a Listowel festival fourth last September; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Has failed to make the frame in six starts since a Listowel festival fourth, hard to fancy

runners.name: Kayamite
runners.spotlight: Has failed to reach the first four in 11 attempts, recent AW form is unconvincing.
runners.comment: Has failed to reach the first four in 11 attempts, recent AW form is unconvincing

runners.name: Uncle Albert
runners.spotlight: No success since Cork (7f, heavy) in March 2024; continues to drop in the ratings, and is now 20lb lower than his winning mark; two runs this term not exactly persuasive; a top apprentice booked.
runners.comment: Continues to drop in the ratings, well treated on old form, a top apprentice booked

runners.name: Rodeeve
runners.spotlight: Career-best was in a C&D maiden two seasons ago; nothing of note last season or on return at Dundalk 12 days ago; chekpieces for the first time now.
runners.comment: Career-best was in a C&D maiden two seasons ago, nothing of note last season or on return

runners.name: Hell Left Loose
runners.spotlight: Heavily raced since winning easily for this rider here last October; won again at Dundalk the following month; unplaced on his next six Dundalk starts before hinting at a return to form in two outings at the Curragh; very closely matched with Miss Americana on running here 13 days ago; definite chance.
runners.comment: Very closely matched with Miss Americana on running here 13 days ago, definite chance

runners.name: Gobi Star
runners.spotlight: Very weak form (turf/AW) since last July; sole turf win was in a 5f maiden in 2022; not an obvious contender.
runners.comment: Very weak form (turf/AW) since last July, sole turf win was in a 5f maiden in 2022
runners.owner: Patrick J Digan & Ms Yvonne Hennessy

runners.name: Zephron
runners.spotlight: Best known as a three-time Curragh winner; five wins in all, none since April 2024; weak form last season; well supported at Navan (6f) on seasonal/stable debut but was always behind and finished last; signs of a return to form  here on latest but held by Miss Americana and Hell Let Loose.
runners.comment: Showed signs of a return here on latest but held by Miss Americana and Hell Let Loose

runners.name: Deluca Chop
runners.spotlight: The most recent of his four wins was at Dundalk in 2023; he has shown consistent AW form lately, placing in his last four races; effective on soft ground in France early in his career; third reserve.
runners.comment: Third reserve, placed in his last four races at Dundalk, effective on soft in France

runners.name: Ethereal Star
runners.spotlight: Best run last year came on final start when third over a trip just short of 1m4f at Killarney; never counted over a shorter trip here on seasonal debut; this distance should suit better; top jockey sticks with here.
runners.comment: Never counted over a shorter trip here on seasonal debut, this distance should suit better

runners.name: Miss Australie
runners.spotlight: Ran well in a Curragh maiden last June; handicap form has been largely disappointing; tailed off on final AW start last year; soundly beaten in sixth behind today's ribal Numidia before that.
runners.comment: Tailed off on final AW start last year, soundly beaten in sixth behind Numidia before that

runners.name: Numidia
runners.spotlight: More effective at Dundalk; not beaten far in fourth in a 2m handicap there on latest; 1-27 record on turf is a negative.
runners.comment: More effective at Dundalk, fourth there on latest, 1-27 record on turf is a negative

runners.name: Bhean Saibhre
runners.spotlight: Generally dependable dual-purpose mare; stayed on well for a 4l third behind today's rivals Idomything and You Make Me Smile over extended 1m4f at Bellewstown (soft) last October; well fancied when third on seasonal debut at Navan; should be a bit sharper now and can uphold form with Mystic Rose and You Make Me Smile.
runners.comment: Fair third when well fancied on seasonal debut, You Make Smile and Mystic Rose behind then

runners.name: Royal Belief
runners.spotlight: Modest on the Flat last year; ordinary form over hurdles until a much-improved display when winning a maiden at Thurles at the end of last month; interesting with Colin Keane booked for his Flat start since joining Cian Collins.
runners.comment: Much-improved when winning a maiden hurdle at Thurles, interesting with Colin Keane booked

runners.name: Prom Queen
runners.spotlight: Career-best when runner-up in a 1m2f Navan handicap last October, though beaten 5l; made a lot of the running before fading to fourth over a shorter trip here 13 days ago on seasonal debut; should strip fitter now and the longer distance may suit
runners.comment: Can build on her recent seasonal debut at his venue, closely matched with Masego

runners.name: Fianna
runners.spotlight: Placed once in seven starts for Willie McCreery; her new trainer appears to have a better chance with Bhean Saibhre; tongue-tie for the first time
runners.comment: Placed once, her new trainer appears to have a better chance with Bhean Saibhre

runners.name: Almenfee
runners.spotlight: Won a 1m4f handicap on good ground 12 months ago in Germany; has made no impact in four starts for this stable; held by Mystic Rose on running at Bellewstown 11 days ago; first reserve.
runners.comment: First reserve, ex-German 1m4f winner, has made no impact in four starts for this stable

runners.name: Ethelwulf
runners.spotlight: Had one good run for the Gosdens, namely a Yarmouth second; moderate form at Dundalk; better here 13 days ago but has a bit to find with Prom Queen and Masego.
runners.comment: Not a bad effort here last time but has a bit to find with Prom Queen and Masego

runners.name: You Make Me Smile
runners.spotlight: Won four races last year, two over hurdles and two on the Flat, including her last start in this sphere at Galway in October; late-season form also included a Bellewstown second, splitting two of these rivals Idomything and Bhean Sainbhre; needs to reverse the form of seasonal debut at Navan with Bhean Saibhre
runners.comment: In top form last October, needs to reverse the form of seasonal debut with Bhean Saibhre
runners.owner: T & R Hennessy & A Fahy Partnership

runners.name: Idomything
runners.spotlight: Beat a subsequent hurdle/Flat winner You Make Me Smile at Bellewstown (1m4f, soft) last October to record her first win at the 11th attempt; down the field in three subsequent races before a break.
runners.comment: Down the field in three races after beating You Make Me Smile at Bellewstown last October

runners.name: Masego
runners.spotlight: Showed some promise when fourth in a juvenile hurdle at Listowel last September; never far from the pace when fifth over an extended 1m1f here 13 days ago on return; ended up only 3/4l behind the pace-setting Prom Queen.
runners.comment: Weak form last year, better when fifth here on seasonal debut, 3/4l behind Prom Queen
runners.owner: Barrow Vale Syndicate/A D Mullins/Ms Margaret Mullins

runners.name: Haeret In Pectore
runners.spotlight: Winner over 7f in Poland in 2024; poor turf form in Ireland last year; showed improvement when switched to AW at Dundalk, putting in satisfactory efforts over 7f and an extended 1m2f before a poor run when well fancied last time; question mark about his stamina; second reserve,
runners.comment: Second reserve, 7f winner in Poland, weak turf form for this yard, a little better on AW

runners.name: Bynx
runners.spotlight: Useful jumper, recorded a third win over hurdles at Thurles last October on her final start before a winter break; also won over fences at Wexford last August; 0-19 on the Flat but had fair form in staying handicaps last season; could pick up a race like this at some stage; trainer also runs Ethereal Star.
runners.comment: Four-time jumps winner, 0-19 on the Flat, fair placed form in staying handicap last season

runners.name: Mystic Rose
runners.spotlight: Maiden has another run under her belt since finishing behind Bhean Saibhre at Navan on seasonal debut; needs to improve from her Bellewstown fourth.
runners.comment: Maiden has another run under her belt since finishing behind Bhean Saibhre at Navan

races.race_name: Best Dressed Competitions Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Crest Of Stars
runners.spotlight: 2m maiden hurdle (good) winner here in January; disappointed on heavy ground over C&D after but bounced back when a staying-on third on handicap debut at Ludlow (2m, good) last time, suggesting he'd be worth another try at this trip; respected back in a novice with Chad Bament claiming 7lb.
runners.comment: Course winner who shaped last time as though this step back up in trip would suit; player.

runners.name: Kilnew Supreme
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in an Irish 3m point behind a subsequent good hurdle winner; has since shaped with promise in a Wincanton bumper and maiden hurdle (1m7f; good) for this stable, the latter following wind surgery; not beaten far on any of those occasions and there could be a good bit of improvement forthcoming now going up in trip.
runners.comment: Runner-up all three starts, but this step up in distance looks sure to suit; big shout.

runners.name: Parchment
runners.spotlight: French hurdle winner (2m1f, very soft) in June, but he disappointed when sent off a short-priced favourite on stable debut at Kempton (2m, good) in November; off since but he's been given wind surgery and it would be no surprise should he leave that effort behind.
runners.comment: French hurdle winner; wind op may spark improvement after a disappointing stable debut.

runners.name: Royal Rocket
runners.spotlight: Showed some promise in a Chepstow bumper (soft) last month but he weakened markedly upped in trip on hurdle debut there (2m7f, good to soft) since, when running too freely; drop back in trip may suit but likely needs more time and experience before showing his best.
runners.comment: Drops in trip but likely needs more time and experience before showing his best.

runners.name: Warfleet
runners.spotlight: Showed promise when runner-up in one of three bumpers in 2024 but he's only managed the two runs since, pulling up on hurdle debut last March and finishing tailed off when returning from an absence 18 days ago for new yard.
runners.comment: Has pulled up and finished tailed off on both hurdle runs, latest back from an absence.

runners.name: Winning Paddy
runners.spotlight: 1-11 in Irish points but his recent efforts have been uninspiring back from an absence and he changed hands for £2,000 in March; faces a tough task on stable/rules debut.
runners.comment: 1-11 in Irish points; faces a tough task on British/stable debut switching to hurdles.

runners.name: Docteur Romeo
runners.spotlight: Well held in his sole bumper, in December 2024, and he's offered little in two starts over hurdles (2m-2m2f), both on good to soft, since returning from a long absence.
runners.comment: Has offered little in two hurdle runs since returning from a long absence.

races.race_name: Royal Ascot Lunch Saturday 20 June Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Pooley's Promise
runners.spotlight: 2lb lower than for Ffos Las win (3m, good) for Ryan Potter last May; soft ground might be blamed for two heavy defeats late last year and perhaps she needed the race when unable to justify market prominence on recent stable debut behind two of these over C&D last month; wore cheekpieces last time and also tongue tied today; interesting to see if her backers return.
runners.comment: Just 3-1 when behind two of these on stable debut here last time; now tongue tied as well

runners.name: Border Gem
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden but she's a reliable sort, runner-up at Hereford (2m4f, good) last time, and looks worth a crack at this sort of trip.
runners.comment: 0-9 but she's a reliable sort and looks worth a crack at this sort of trip

runners.name: Lady Caro
runners.spotlight: 2-16 in handicaps, raised to 3m here (good) for last three outings; ridden by Cameron Johnstone-Baker last month on last two starts, runner-up before winning easily with a career best four weeks ago (Miss Tiara third); 8lb higher today but needs plenty of respect.
runners.comment: Cameron Johnstone-Baker rode over C&D (good) when winning easily with career best; up 8lb

runners.name: Jena d'Oudairies
runners.spotlight: Down the weights but lack of impact in six handicaps (two over this sort of trip) prevents any confidence in her taking advantage; has worn tongue-tie and cheekpieces in recent starts, which are now replaced with blinkers.
runners.comment: Lack of impact in six handicaps, two over this sort of trip; different equipment this time

runners.name: Hurst Hill
runners.spotlight: Opened account at Newton Abbot (2m6f, good) last July and runner-up in three of her four starts since, at up to 3m3f; on the premises on that evidence but the 163 days off ask a question.
runners.comment: On the premises on 2025 evidence but the 163 days off ask a question

runners.name: Miss Tiara
runners.spotlight: Had wind surgery (her second) before penultimate start; reportedly made a respiratory noise that day but performed much better when staying-on third of ten to Lady Caro over C&D (good) last time, albeit making it look very hard work in comparison with the winner; has 9lb swing in the weights for a near 7l beating.
runners.comment: Staying-on 3rd to Lady Caro over C&D, albeit making it look very hard work in comparison

runners.name: Bee's Kiss
runners.spotlight: Won a good-ground Irish point last May but heavy defeats in her four hurdle races; now goes handicapping with a big rethink on trip (last three races were at about 2m) but also from 8lb out of the handicap proper.
runners.comment: Heavy defeats as hurdler; goes handicapping with a big rethink on trip but also 8lb wrong

runners.name: Poet's Muse
runners.spotlight: Won a 3m maiden point last May; beaten 56l, 46l and 63l this year in her three hurdle races (at up to an extended 2m5f) so does not have any form claims, and she reportedly bled from the nose last time; steps back up in trip for this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Won a 3m maiden point last May; beaten 56l, 46l and 63l in her three hurdle races

runners.name: Forever Penywern
runners.spotlight: Signed off for William Haggas with a brace of selling wins (1m3f/1m2f) on the Flat late last summer; three goes over hurdles but his fair third at Stratford (about 2m2f, soft) in March wasn't matched either side of it; will need to have his Stratford hat on for this handicap debut but it's still early days and that's possible.
runners.comment: One encouraging run and two poor ones over hurdles; handicap debut in this sphere

runners.name: Dirty Den
runners.spotlight: Hooded when solid third over C&D (good) at the end of last year and scored at Plumpton (about 2m4f, soft) on next start in February; revised mark has seen him held in two subsequent starts but that doesn't tell the whole story as they were seriously competitive series qualifiers; cheekpieces (worn once over hurdles when second here 13 months ago) are reapplied and he needs a firm second look.
runners.comment: Not disgraced in tough handicaps of late; can be competitive in reapplied cheekpieces

runners.name: Best Night
runners.spotlight: 22-1 when she made a winning stable debut in Newton Abbot novice (2m1f) in September and was a respectable fourth of 13 in C&D handicap (also good to soft) in January; doubled her tally when scoring at Huntingdon (2m, good) last month but it was a tired mistake at the last when 15l second a fortnight later; still needs considering.
runners.comment: Won penultimate start; tired mistake exacerbated defeat latest; needs considering

runners.name: Public Enemy
runners.spotlight: Won good-ground maiden and novice hurdles last spring but he hasn't fired in handicaps, including in a tongue-tie on his last two starts and with added cheekpieces (now swapped for a visor) at Plumpton a fortnight ago; handicapper is trying to help but others look safer.
runners.comment: Wheels have come off since handicapping; gets first-time visor from reduced mark

runners.name: Green Sky
runners.spotlight: Beaten just 2l when fourth at Newton Abbot (2m1f, good) earlier in the month but Friday's heavy defeat on similar ground at Exeter (extended 2m2f) takes some explaining; swaps cheekpieces for blinkers and her yard might have better prospects with Best Night.
runners.comment: Heavy defeat on Friday; blinkers need to prompt a massive turnaround

runners.name: Myrighthandman
runners.spotlight: 3m Irish point winner (favourite) who recorded similar RPRs in three maiden hurdles (about 2m, good to soft/good) across February and March; point win and pedigree suggest this step up in trip should suit; of obvious interest on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: 3m point winner who's up in trip for handicap debut; pedigree suggests it will suit too

runners.name: Taritino
runners.spotlight: Landed a 'hands and heels' event over C&D in March and respectable efforts in two subsequent starts, latterly when second off this mark at Exeter (about 2m2f, also good; Green Sky tailed off) on Friday; on the premises again if this doesn't come too soon.
runners.comment: Solid second on Friday and on the premises again if this doesn't come too soon

runners.name: Midnight Pass
runners.spotlight: Finding her feet over hurdles and October's third at Hereford (2m, good) when last seen was quite encouraging; pedigree suggests this new trip can unlock more and she's one to be interested in on this handicap debut despite the absence.
runners.comment: Encouraging third when last seen in October; new trip can suit; respected on h'cap debut

runners.name: Bobbarelli
runners.spotlight: 9yo who was 2-12 in points (the wins coming over 2m4f on good/good to firm); weakened on February's belated hurdling debut (soft) and couldn't convert what turned into a golden opportunity at Warwick (2m5f, good) four weeks ago; handicap debut and may yet do better in this sphere.
runners.comment: Golden opportunity was missed last time; now makes handicap debut so 9yo may yet do better

runners.name: Saucats
runners.spotlight: Longstanding maiden over hurdles who hasn't done enough to suggest he's about to shed his maiden tag, finishing 17.5l adrift of Little Lady Lucy at Wincanton (1m7f, good) last time; has a bit to find on the revised terms but not ruled out for minor honours.
runners.comment: Longstanding maiden who has a bit to find with Little Lady Lucy, but could make the frame.

runners.name: Little Lady Lucy
runners.spotlight: Showed little in three runs at around 2m4f this season before finally opening her account over hurdles at the 12th attempt, when a switch to front-running tactics and a drop in trip at Wincanton (1m7f, good) ten days ago brought about the decisive breakthrough; subsequent penalty may not be enough to prevent another bold showing if she can replicate the effort.
runners.comment: Gained a breakthrough win in good style and the penalty may not prevent another bold bid.

runners.name: Portcammon
runners.spotlight: Has dropped to a competitive mark on the pick of his form, but recent efforts have been underwhelming and the stable looks to have stronger claims with Little Lady Lucy; could come good at some point, but needs to turn things around.
runners.comment: May come good at some point but stable has a stronger contender in Little Lady Lucy.

runners.name: Miss Fedora
runners.spotlight: Signs of a revival when fourth over fences at Fakenham (2m5f) in January, before 3m5f stretched her (refused at the final fence) on final start for Seamus Mullins; offered little when sent off joint-favourite on stable debut last month; tongue-tie wore then quickly dispensed with and she reverts to hurdles on a workable mark, but with plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Out of form over fences, but reverts to hurdles on a fair mark if she can find some spark.

runners.name: Most Wanted
runners.spotlight: Won over a mile on the Flat last summer but has yet to transfer that ability to hurdles, last seen pulling up at Plumpton (2m, good to soft) in October on handicap hurdle debut; returns from a 184-day break off 3lb lower, but still with a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Yet to fire over hurdles and returns from a 184-day break with something to prove.

runners.name: Galactic Jack
runners.spotlight: A useful middle-distance Flat performer, but still a maiden over hurdles after seven attempts and has yet to rediscover his form in two starts since returning from a long absence this season; likely best watched for now.
runners.comment: 0-7 over hurdles and has yet to find his form following a long absence; best watched.

runners.name: Old Bridge
runners.spotlight: 0-13 over hurdles and has struggled a little with consistency in the past, with his better efforts coming over further; however, he went close off a similar mark at Hereford (2m3f, good) in November and, despite the drop back in trip and returning from a break, he still warrants respect.
runners.comment: Went close to breaking his duck in November; worth considering down in trip after a break.

runners.name: Daring To Dream
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in her bumpers but had only hinted at that ability in three novice hurdles before stepping up on handicap debut when a keeping-on third from off the pace at Stratford (2m, good) last month; open to further progress and one to consider off 1lb lower.
runners.comment: Shaped well when a staying-on third on handicap debut latest and open to further progress.

runners.name: Pillar The Poet
runners.spotlight: Finished a remote fourth over 2m3f here in February before taking a backward step when tailed off at Exeter (extended 2m5f, good) two weeks ago when tried in a hood (retained), both times ridden by a 7lb claimer; still lightly raced but needs to step up considerably.
runners.comment: Well held on both hurdle starts for this stable; needs to step up considerably to figure.

runners.name: Mi Sueno
runners.spotlight: Dual soft-ground hurdle winner (handles good) in early 2024 but has struggled for form since, including on both starts for this stable; latest C&D effort offered a glimmer of encouragement and he's well treated on old form, but he needs another step forward today.
runners.comment: Latest C&D run offered some encouragement and he's well treated, but he needs to step up.

runners.name: Blazing Gaden
runners.spotlight: Has offered little in four runs over hurdles (soft/heavy), including on last month's handicap debut; needs the better ground to spark significant improvement.
runners.comment: Has shown little in four runs over hurdles so far, but the better ground may help.

runners.name: Gasmani
runners.spotlight: Came good as a hurdler (sixth attempt) when asserting by 5l in a five-runner and four-finisher novice handicap over C&D (good) 13 days ago, having breezed into the lead before two out under Charlie Maggs; hot favourite Noble Blue unseated early in that race; up 6lb but probably has a good chance.
runners.comment: Well on top in a five-runner novice handicap over C&D (good) 13 days ago; raised 6lb

runners.name: Sage Green
runners.spotlight: Fell mid-race on chasing debut last month; better 18 days later on second attempt in that sphere (albeit last of three) and peak hurdles efforts make this look an attractive mark, but they were all at about 2m and this sort of trip is totally new under rules for this 2024 Irish 3m point winner.
runners.comment: This sort of trip is totally new under rules for this 2024 Irish 3m point winner

runners.name: Noble Blue
runners.spotlight: 0-14 under rules but 6-9 in points; second in two handicap hurdles over about 2m6f at Exeter this year, which saw him start 11-10 favourite for five-runner race over C&D (good) 13 days ago but he clipped heels and unseated in the early stages; that was the race won by Gasmani; should give him a lot more to think about this time, barring mishaps.
runners.comment: 0-14 under rules, 6-9 in points; twice 2nd over 2m6f before clipping heals in Gasmani race

runners.name: Holeshot
runners.spotlight: Chasing on last five starts has not really worked out; Taunton (2m3f) last February and Warwick (2m) last March saw him register a double over hurdles and he's back to his last winning mark for today's switch in codes but, after three non-completions at about 3m over fences, stamina also has to be proved.
runners.comment: Back to last winning mark for today's switch from chasing; stamina has to be proved

runners.name: Sam's Amour
runners.spotlight: Triple hurdle winner (2m2f-2m5f) in 2024 but she struggled in her final three runs last year (reportedly bled from the nose on chase debut final time) and did so again when returned to action 18 days ago; now 7lb below her peak mark.
runners.comment: Struggled in her final three runs last year and when returned to action 18 days ago

runners.name: Mcgregors Charge
runners.spotlight: A chase at Perth (3m, good) last August off 5lb lower was his only win from 17 starts; left Ella Pickard before pulled up here 13 days ago on debut for new stable, but it's notable that he started 13-8 favourite in that chase; now he's back hurdling for first time since 2022.
runners.comment: Pulled up 13 days ago for new stable, but it's notable he was 13-8 favourite in that chase

runners.name: Final Entry
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 5yo, absent 473 days before this season; showed some ability in 2024, so he's not totally ruled out over today's very new trip, despite being well held in his two runs (one on Flat) this year; tongue-tie (worn twice on Flat in Ireland at start of career) returns.
runners.comment: Absent 473 days before this season; not totally ruled out over today's very new trip

races.race_name: Dorset & Somerset Air Ambulance Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Modern Style
runners.spotlight: Well-bred 4yo who improved markedly switched to handicaps when a wide-margin winner at Exeter (extended 2m, good) five days ago; the manner of the win suggests there's more to come; faces a quick turnaround and stamina to prove up in trip, but he looks well treated under a penalty and is the one to beat.
runners.comment: Wide-margin winner on handicap debut last week and the one to beat under a penalty.

runners.name: Lake Tiberias
runners.spotlight: Hasn't quite built on a promising bumper debut since going over hurdles, including in a couple of handicaps; however, his recent 17l third when attracting support at Wincanton (2m4f, good) was more encouraging; easing in the weights and one to consider in a modest contest.
runners.comment: Offered more when third at Wincanton last time and one to consider in a modest contest.

runners.name: No Mean Feat
runners.spotlight: 0-14 over hurdles and has shown little in three runs (2m-2m4f) since returning from a long absence; cheekpieces tried last time quickly dispensed with; plenty to prove.
runners.comment: 0-14 over hurdles and cheekpieces tried last time quickly dispensed with; plenty to prove.

runners.name: Athena's Dream
runners.spotlight: Has yet to build on the promise of her bumper debut since switched to hurdles and was beaten 29l when tried at 2m4f and in blinkers at Wincanton last time on handicap debut; still early days, but needs to find improvement.
runners.comment: Well held when tried at 2m4f and in blinkers latest and needs to find improvement.

runners.name: Tobi Kanobee
runners.spotlight: Put in a bold bid from the front at Newton Abbot (extended 2m5f, good) last week before fading into a remote third late; remains on a tough mark and others have achieved more.
runners.comment: Shaped with more encouragement last time, but more needed off this sort of mark.

runners.name: Future Times
runners.spotlight: Longstanding maiden who didn't run badly on first two starts back from a mammoth absence; out of the weights but not hard to see him hitting the frame in a weak contest, if bouncing back from a poor effort last time.
runners.comment: Out of the weights but could hit the frame, if bouncing back from a poor run last time.

runners.name: Ritaan
runners.spotlight: Fair Flat maiden but yet to progress over hurdles and has often raced too keenly, though was a bit more tractable back at 2m last time without ever getting involved; hood removed now back up in trip and may do better at some point, but she remains a work in progress for now.
runners.comment: Free-going filly who may do better in time but looks a work in progress for now.

runners.name: Peace In The Park
runners.spotlight: Encouraging second on handicap debut at Huntingdon (2m, heavy), but that form has taken some knocks and he's been well held twice since on quicker ground; on a competitive mark but needs to bounce back..
runners.comment: On a competitive mark but needs to rediscover his initial handicap promise.

runners.name: Forest Hills
runners.spotlight: 1m2f Flat winner who has yet to show anything of note over hurdles, including on handicap debut at Wincanton (1m7f, good) ten days ago; needs this step up in trip and the tongue-tie to spark considerable improvement.

runners.owner: 25/26 Dennis C H Yip Trainer Syndicate (2)

runners.name: Double Money
runners.spotlight: 2-3 when based in Australia but a down-the-field outsider on first two Hong Kong starts (both 6f); drops back in trip today.
runners.comment: 2-3 when based in Australia but a down-the-field outsider on first two Hong Kong starts

runners.name: Sky Cap
runners.spotlight: Resurgent 4yo who has won twice over 6f here in recent weeks, holding on well on each occasion; moves back up in grade today but should be okay back at 5f and Joao Moreira is booked again.
runners.comment: Resurgent 4yo who has won twice over 6f here in recent weeks; back up in grade today

runners.name: Bunta Baby
runners.spotlight: Progressive 5yo who has won three of his last four over C&D; evidently in prime form but combination of stall 11 and latest 7lb rise demands more from him.
runners.comment: Won three of his last five over C&D; stall 11 makes things awkward today, though
runners.owner: Chu Tak Wing, Casey Chu Yin Ling & Ronald Chu Tak Wah

runners.name: Candlelight Dinner
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner this season; arguably posted career-best effort when close third to Bunta Baby here last month, but had stall 1 that day and faces tough task from double-digit draw this time.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner this season; placed off this mark last month but drawn much wider today

runners.name: Copper Core
runners.spotlight: 1-2 in Australia; has gradually improved in local trials and is drawn on the inside for this official Hong Kong debut, but probably best watched all the same.
runners.comment: 1-2 in Australia; well drawn for Hong Kong debut but probably best watched all the same

runners.name: Central Bank
runners.spotlight: 2-7 when based in Australia; finished down the field after racing too freely on Hong Kong debut at Sha Tin (5f) in February; needs to take a big step forward here.
runners.comment: Finished down the field after racing too freely on Hong Kong debut in February

runners.name: High Praise
runners.spotlight: Reserve; 1-5 in Australia, the win gained in a 1m maiden in March 2025; makes Hong Kong debut after six-month break and may find 5f too sharp.
runners.comment: Makes Hong Kong debut after six-month break and may find 5f too sharp
runners.owner: Anthony Poon Tak Ching, Vivica Poon Ip Sze Man et al

runners.name: Violet Star
runners.spotlight: Placed for first time in Hong Kong when running-on third over 1m here a fortnight ago, and his British form suggests this move back up in trip will suit; one to consider.
runners.comment: Ran on well for third over 1m here a fortnight ago and today's longer trip will suit

runners.name: Packing Fighter
runners.spotlight: Yet to build upon his promising Hong Kong debut in January but looked unlucky not to finish closer than fifth at Sha Tin (1m1f; repeatedly short of room in straight) last month and may not have been suited by dirt when down the field last time; a possible.
runners.comment: Unlucky not to finish closer than fifth 2 runs ago and dirt may not have suited last time

runners.name: Excellence Value
runners.spotlight: Now 5lb lower than when winning over C&D for Mark Newnham in October, but this year's form for Chris So has been very disappointing and he needs to turn a corner.
runners.comment: C&D winner in October but badly out of form for new stable this year

runners.name: The Auspicious
runners.spotlight: Three wins this season (7f/1m/1m3f); just a respectable sixth at Sha Tin (1m2f) this month but recent 2lb drop helps; can make his presence felt.
runners.comment: Three wins this season; just a respectable sixth last time but current mark is workable

runners.name: Mission Giant
runners.spotlight: Won when trained in Australia but was a well-beaten outsider on recent Hong Kong debut (1m).
runners.comment: Won when trained in Australia but was a well-beaten outsider on recent Hong Kong debut

runners.name: Ace War
runners.spotlight: Started off for Mark Newnham with two emphatic Class 4 course wins last month (1m3f/1m1f); moves up in grade today and Zac Purton will be 2lb overweight, but still commands respect in current mood.
runners.comment: Started off for Mark Newnham with two emphatic Class 4 course wins last month; up in grade

runners.name: Highland Rahy
runners.spotlight: Yet to win this season but gave pretty good account when fifth over C&D last month and Britney Wong, who rode a double on Sunday, claims 7lb today; could have a say.
runners.comment: Yet to win this season but gave pretty good account when fifth over C&D last month
runners.owner: Peter Chu Ka Lok, Maria Teresa Chu & Dr C Chu Ping Wing

runners.name: Nezha
runners.spotlight: Came good in Hong Kong with 1m dirt win last month, his seventh attempt; not beaten far when seventh on that surface three weeks ago but needs to transfer his recent improvement back to turf.
runners.comment: Won on dirt last month but needs to transfer that improvement back to turf

runners.name: So You Will
runners.spotlight: 0-5 in Hong Kong but made the frame over 1m on last two turf starts and was close fifth on recent dirt debut; shapes as though this extra furlong will suit and has the assistance of Joao Moreira today; major player.
runners.comment: Knocking on the door in recent weeks ago Joao Moreira is booked today; major player

runners.name: Mighty Strength
runners.spotlight: Caused 55-1 shock on seasonal debut at Sha Tin (1m1f) in October and ran best race since when close fourth there (1m2f) this month, but he's drawn wide here and his overall profile remains unconvincing.
runners.comment: Made the frame at Sha Tin this month but overall profile remains unconvincing
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Li Ming On, Joyce Li Lok Yan & Henry Li Lok Him

runners.name: China Win
runners.spotlight: In-form 4yo who won twice over this trip in January and was placed on both outings last month (1m1f/1m); not yet fully exposed after only ten runs; respected.
runners.comment: Placed twice since pair of 1m1f wins in January and not yet fully exposed; respected

runners.name: Liveandletlive
runners.spotlight: In the frame on eight of his ten appearances this season, most recently when close second over 1m2f at Sha Tin this month; holds no secrets from the handicapper but is likely to be right in the thick of things again.
runners.comment: Largely consistent 5yo who has made the frame on eight on his ten appearances this season

runners.name: Fallon
runners.spotlight: Reserve; kept on well to exploit reduced mark over C&D last month, his fifth Hong Kong win, but safely held at Sha Tin since and will probably remain vulnerable.
runners.comment: C&D winner off reduced mark last month but safely held since; probably remains vulnerable

runners.name: Love Together
runners.spotlight: Opened Hong Kong account with 5f course win in January and was placed over that trip on next outing; safely held over C&D twice since but had tough draw last time and may yet have untapped potential over this trip.
runners.comment: 5f course winner this year; had tough draw over C&D last time; still unexposed over 6f

runners.name: Wings Of War
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far on any of his five C&D starts this year and continues to edge down the weights, but has a modest strike-rate and others appeal more.
runners.comment: Not beaten far on any of five C&D starts this year but has modest strike-rate

runners.name: Packing Glory
runners.spotlight: Won over 5f on debut in November and has been placed on three of his five 6f/7f starts since; didn't get home after racing too freely over 7f this month, but this drop back in trip looks the right move and he can be dangerous from stall 1; blinkers the headgear of choice today.
runners.comment: Placed on three of his five 6f/7f starts since 5f win on debut; big player from stall 1
runners.owner: Lee Wan Keung & Lee Wong Wai Kuen

runners.name: Meowth
runners.spotlight: Reserve; placed on five of his six starts since Class 4 C&D win in November, the last two in this higher grade; will have obvious each-way claims if he gets a run.
runners.comment: Placed on five of six starts since Class 4 C&D win in November; obvious each-way claims

runners.name: Masterofmyuniverse
runners.spotlight: Won four times over this trip at Sha Tin last season; yet to hit top gear this term but was denied a clear run when down the field over C&D last month and has become well handicapped.
runners.comment: Yet to hit top gear this season but had an excuse last time and is well handicapped now

runners.name: Kansas
runners.spotlight: Smart and speedy 2yo when based in Ireland but made a low-key Hong Kong debut at Sha Tin (5f) this month.
runners.comment: Smart 2yo when based in Ireland but made low-key Hong Kong debut last month

runners.name: Jubilant Winner
runners.spotlight: Three Sha Tin wins this season, the latest in this grade in February; had to settle for fourth off today's mark there last month but could still have a bigger run in him.
runners.comment: Three wins this season; only fourth off this mark last time but could have more to offer

runners.name: Without Rhyme
runners.spotlight: Won 6f maiden handicap on debut in Australia, but this Hong Kong debut comes after an 11-month absence and he's poorly drawn; tongue-tie and cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Won on debut in Australia last year but has poor draw for this Hong Kong debut

runners.name: Flying Fortress
runners.spotlight: Went close over 1m on consecutive appearances towards end of last year, but has been well below that form lately and is drawn on the outside for this return to sprinting.
runners.comment: Well below best over 1m/1m1f lately and poorly drawn for return to sprinting
runners.owner: 23/24 Cody Mo Wai Kit Trainer Syndicate

runners.name: My Day My Way
runners.spotlight: Improved 5yo who led close home to overcome a double-digit draw over C&D last month and is now bidding for a hat-trick; has another wide draw to contend with today, though.
runners.comment: Has won his last two over C&D but has another wide draw to contend with

runners.name: Aurio
runners.spotlight: Two wins for new stable this season, the latest a career-best performance over C&D last month; another 8lb higher today but Zac Purton keeps the ride; likely contender.
runners.comment: Two wins for new stable this season, the latest a career-best effort over C&D last month

runners.name: Greater Bae
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who scored over C&D in January and lost out only by a neck to My Day My Way here last month; deserted by Zac Purton and not ideally drawn today but still considered.
runners.comment: Low-mileage C&D winner; went close here last month; one to consider

runners.name: Sovereign Fund
runners.spotlight: Returned to form with C&D win on stable debut in February, but could not land a telling blow off new mark on either start since; needs to kick on again.
runners.comment: Won stable debut in February but unable to land a telling blow on either start since
runners.owner: Glenn Chan Wai Cheung & Raymond Chan Ka Shun
